It's like asking why a screwdriver isn't good at sawing wood, or calling C a terrible language because it's hard to make CRUD apps with it. i mean eventually then will, losing means open source, vertically integrated hardware means you can opensource and win on cost Looks like 3.6 Flash is the first model with their newest pretraining run (cutoff date is 2026/03), long after 2.5 series. This is the darnest thing - all the testimonies are just jepgs? Did they run out of time to work on the css? IMO Gemini has the best free tier models/app for everyday use. Muse-Spark is perhaps just slightly better, but has none of the connectivity to my GApps (for things like “create a recipe in my Google Docs from this image”).Plus they are probably running these things on every Google search so saving tokens is a huge win for them. Free plan, the default tier without requiring a subscription. If you use through the Gemini App or gemini.google without paying anything, the model used is 3.6 Flash.Rankings for text are here https://arena.ai/leaderboard/textFor comparison of Free Tiers: - Gemini serves 3.6-flash (rank 12) - ChatGPT serves 5.5-Instant (rank 23) - Claude serves Sonnet 5 (rank 27) - Meta AI serves muse-spark-1.1 (rank 5)While Meta AI serves the better ranked model, it doesn't end up working that well for other things. For example, if I ask "help me buy a new raincoat", it ends up suggesting a Cambodian website, whereas Google is well integrated with Google shopping. It doesn't have the same integration with GApps outside of Gmail/Calendar. A few other email connectors are available.Claude has one of the best interfaces with connectors, skills, and plugins galore, but the model and limits are restrictive on the free tier.Gemini, as far as I know, I've never hit a rate limit on Flash.I believe Gemini is going to gain market share through the free tier funnel while serving models as cost-effectively as possible. People are going to use Gemini because they use GApps and Google.ChatGPT and Anthropic are going to be competing for the API/Business users, but for everyone else they are going have to become Google before Google becomes them. So 3.6 Flash is a somewhat of an admission that Google miscalculated by charging 3-5x for 3.5 Flash what it did for 3.0 Flash (3x input and output costs plus large token inefficiency changes) despite only modest improvements?3.5 Flash Lite is only a hair cheaper than 3.0 Flash, but I think 3.0 Flash is a massively more capable model? Pretty underwhelming, as expected honestly. I don't want to know what morale is like at DeepMind right now. Especially when Google owns 15% of anthropic and serves them compute. Double especially when your boss (Hassibis) is also an early investor in Anthropic. Hell his NW might be more Anthropic than Google. Yep, agreed. They still are not releasing anything frontier-class (Gemini Pro) at this point. Feels to me that they keep getting scooped by others (e.g. Kimi 3) and then are retrenching. It does seem like their releases are getting closer together. I get the feeling they realized they were trying to roll out to their entire ecosystem and now they’re focusing more just directly on the AI model itself. I think give it a little time and they’ll start to be one of the competitors too. Here's the issue:GLM 5.2 is better, also cheaper, and almost as fast.So essentially, a big L for Google. Combine this with them not being able to produce a frontier model this generation... hmm implications Counter-point, cost per task is almost the same ($0.47 vs $0.50) between GLM 5.2 and Gemini 3.6 Flash. [0] Not to mention the subscription plans likely produce 10x value compared to GLM 5.2 API, unsure the rate limits on a equal subscription vs subscription, but Google subscriptions offer tons of other value, including 1 year of Free Gemini for Education accounts.[1] https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flash 3.6 is roughly 50% faster, which isn't totally insignificant for being marginally more expensive.[1][1]artificialanalysis.ai Sure, but there's no sota alternative from Google. That's it, and its beaten by GLM 5.2 on every measure except somewhat speed.I find that quite staggering. GLM is open weights Speed is definitely a marketable quality. All these things are a trade-off and solely measuring against SOTA I don't feel is always helpful. "..and in some benchmarks like DeepSWE by Datacurve, we observe up to 65%, all at a lower cost per output token.""3.6 Flash delivers higher precision with fewer unwanted code edits and reduced execution loops, as seen in DeepSWE (49% vs. 37%)"So which one is it? 65% or 49%? Or you could have read it more closely before posting a comment saying it didn't make sense. You know, the old school way. I'm a big fan of the Flash-Lite models. They're exceedingly fast and deliver great outputs for high volume use cases where you need to process requests at scale. Can't wait to try the newer version. Flash Lite: 0.3/m and 2.5/mDeepseek Pro: 0.435/m 0.87/mThat's wildly ambitious pricing by Google. You can maybe get away with spicy pricing at the SOTA edge but at the lower tiers everything is a lot more price sensitive. You need to compare cost per task buddy boy. Cost per token doesn't tell you much when you don't know how many tokens a model will use to accomplish a task They are working on coming up with a better code name. You know, something like ”Fable” or ”Sol”, gotta have one these days. Personally I think they should go with “Mafia”. How cool would that sound? 3.6 Mafia. Why wouldn't they? Luna isn't a Flash model. OpenAI hasn't released a flash-equivalent model since gpt-oss-120b. Did you ask me a question and then answered it yourself in the very next sentence?Anyway, given that both Gemini and OpenAI have 3 sizes of models, one would think Google compares their medium size to OpenAIs. The benchmarks are not particularly impressive. I suppose they needed to release something since the long pause. But not clear why would I use it now. A lot of disappointment here in the comments, but models like these aren't meant to compete with the likes of Fable or GPT 5.6.I use 3.1 Flash Lite regularly to classify listings on eCommerce websites. It's great for this task - fast, cheap and accurate.In fact, it was the single best model we tried in terms of the speed vs accuracy vs price tradeoffs - including the Chinese models.Of course, 3.5 Flash was more accurate but the 5x cost increase couldn't be justified.3.5 Flash Lite sounds like it could be a strict upgrade for our use case, without a significant increase in costs or drop in speed.It's not GPT-6 but it's not trying to be. It's a completely different tool and great at what it does. I'm going to get downvoted/flagged but I feel like we need a new type of "Show HN/Tell HN" etc for "New AI Model Available".Front page is tedious these days. > I'm going to get downvoted/flagged but [...]Why do you care that much? Just say it. You're letting an imaginary score determine if you should express your suggestion for improvement. That's a bit wild. Models are expensive and low performance. On top of that they make you jump through hoops to even use these models without being throttled even for the weaker models. The only reason we are using them is credits. As soon as credits run out we are switching immediately. the real product is the naming confusion we made along the way. Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, 3.5 Flash Cyber — at this point even the model cards need a model to explain them quite a good model, the speed/price/quality ration is a new golden intersection for me, not sure if its as good as grok 4.5 but quite fast/capable model. It feels like AI is going to be the end of Google. The post-Schmidt company culture cannot produce consistent, consumer-friendly products that any sane person would want to use consistently. "The model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender soon as part of a limited-access pilot program"we are stealing plutocracy from the jaws of emancipation.i don't want to live in a world where abundance is guarded and shared among politicians and cronies, whilst the rest are left to rot. I have no skin in this game and this comment will be gray in a few minutes BUT a friendly reminder that these types of threads are astroturfed heavily by competitor labs and any info should be taken with a massive grain of salt. I remember back when Gemini looked like it was the best model that this comment section was full of confident predictions that Google had "won" and that no one would ever catch up with them again. The most embarassing part is that I kinda believed them. I think it’s safe to say Google seems a bit out of the top AI competition now. The “cyber” stuff also starts to become laughable with open models providing the full power without the crap Anthropic, Google, OpenAI are trying to frontload on you(I.e you are not allowed to develop/review a login system, pay a special cyber operation team to do it for you). They really deserve to become irrelevant in the future of AI. Well, you can use the google models from Pi. Go to aistudio.google.com and set up an API key. There is a free quota, it's relatively large for the Flash Lite models....and last time I looked the limits were more generous for Gemma 4 there, but they have been tightened a bit. That's how it goes, always changing. The silence is deafening.Google watches over the last few months a flat out assault on the Pareto curve from American and Chinese companies. Release after release pushing the boundaries of frontier intelligence and price/performance.And the response from arguably the biggest AI research labs in the world by headcount is Flash 3.6.What do you do when you are given essentially unlimited resources and still find yourself falling behind? I wonder whether this is more the fault of Hassabis or Pichai. They are clearly both less capable than Altman or Amodei. Gemini 3.5 flash is already a pretty good model. But, unfortunately, the primary way you can interact with it for coding is through Antigravity - which is actively developer hostile.It doesn't matter how good the model is if you're (mostly) forced to use it in Antigravity - which turns any model into crap.Wake me up when Antigravity doesn't suck. Plugged 3.5 Flash Lite into an existing agent harness that was previously using 3.1 Flash Lite and this shit just does not work. It's not following instructions and is not producing the correct tool calls. Why exactly are they announcing these completely milquetoast models ?I'd be low-keying the release if anything, given how lame they are compared to their competition.What am I missing? I use 3.5 flash 10x more than any other model, despite have access to all of them. If I'm going to play a slot machine, I'd rather get the pain over with quickly. We're almost five years into the whole GenAI thing and we're still relying on these guys to spoonfeed us incremental updates.It's time for them to start focusing on open-weight models and efficiency. Otherwise there's just a layer of marketing hype and "will it do this?" that has to be cut through for evaluation of each and every release cycle.Models are getting easier and easier to create. The money, if there's any here, is in the harness the user interfaces with, and the data centers running them. Google seems to be falling way behind the pack. antigravity cli is pure trash. gpt 3.5 pro is now behind and isn't released yet. GPT 6 and Fable 6 releasing next month. What the hell is going on over there ? > What the hell is going on over thereGoogle was late to coding agents and as-per-usual fucked it up with their crazy project management culture.Usually Google gets away with it due to inertia, however this time they are paying a heavy price because they missed out on the training data that Anthropic and OpenAI have gathered with claude and codex. > we have taken an intentional approach to deploying 3.5 Flash Cyber. The model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partnersScrew your government! US and Israeli governments should get the least access, but of course we all know they'll be the (only) ones to get full unfiltered access. is it just me or is this one-upping each other every few days getting ridiculous secreting a whiff of desperation? I keep saying this and people dont believe me, but I have b2b saas systems with actual agents running around the clock, and the performance/stability of the flash model is higher than most other models.Meaning, its predictable with tool calls, wont spin off a million tools/do weird behavior, its reasonable. Even sonnet in a real world decision making scenario is not reliable, or will reason so long its incredibly expensive.The benchmarks arent catching all the value, and most people have never actually ran an ai agent in a real context that matters Who's most people? What are you talking about? Most people here use agents every day and I wouldn't trust flash or pro to touch any important project of mine because they're both terrible compared to the competition, waste of time every time I give them a chance I mean like an ai agent doing some sort of HR work, not a coding agent. Very few businesses are trusting an autonomous agent. 3.6 Flash has the same performance on artificial analysis benchmarks as 3.5 Flash. So... what... is... the... point?.. With all the naysayers on Gemini models I'm curious how many people actually use Gemini regularly?For me, Gemini models are the most usable. Claude Opus and Mistral always try to turn queries into one-shot enormous commits, which just burns tokens, time and annoys me for something which is still wrong more often than not.Gemini seems far better at listening to instructions and giving me what I actually want, on top of using far fewer tokens and wasting my time. Fable is the only model that's come close to Gemini Pro for me.And as this is about Flash, it's exciting, I find Flash can usually get the right answer pretty quickly and without too much nonsense. I use all of the major providers daily and I tend to go to Gemini for "fast lookups" where a good enough answer is probably OK. I use ChatGPT and Claude for anything where it matters and generally when I invoke all three -- Gemini is the most surface level with responses, and also sycophantic.It gets worse from there. Gemini is terrible at agentic coding, primarily because Agy is terrible. I noticed Google updated Agy with this release, so perhaps that's finally going in a good direction. I'll have to test it. Thus far, my experience in countless experiments has been Gemini models being 2x faster yet with less depth in their solutions and a lot more going off track.I very rarely have to stop Codex or Claude Code sessions because they're doing something random and unexpected (or not asked for). I genuinely think Gemini models are brilliant but virtually useless in agentic scenarios in my experience of the last few years (2.5, 3, 3.1, 3.5).I should also note that Gemini web UI annoying resets to its lowest intelligence which feels scummy and Google is not transparent about what "extended thinking" really is. Past posts have pointed to "extended" being medium. Every other provider gives you the raw value (medium/high/etc).So honestly, I feel Google would rather I don't use their models. They just want to get a little bit of mindshare and stay in the conversation. I had the Ultra plan and cancelled it once it was apparent they were not improving the agentic experience nor trying to compete. I agree, I see how Gemini itself with a usable harness can be excellent. But in agy with forced eager compaction (~125k with 3.1-pro, ~200k with flash) a kind of laziness and forgetting shows through that leads to an endless sequence of stopgap instructions, even with rigorous GEMINI.md and isolated task delegation and a good task tracking system. Agy is basically useless for more complex problems as far as I am concerned, at least when used somewhat autonomously as one could expect from claude. For strictly mechanical one-shot tasks it might be fine. I've spent way too much time working around these limitations instead of just continuing to use claude. Hoping that things would have improved with flash 3.6 I feel that it's actually worse in following instructions, and always acts even when just asked a question. If just agy offered a better experience and got rid of the terrible forced automatic eager compaction.PS: That opus-4.6 via agy works so much better points in another direction though! not a google fanboy by any stretch... though i've been thrilled with the flash line of models... i exclusively use it on high, and have found it to be a great fit for increasing productivity 10-fold while maintaining quality... sure it can't just go off and one-shot a bunch of work, but at the complexity level i tend to work at, neither can the frontier in a robust way that i can be confident in... sure i have to be in the loop more, but that helps keep me grounded and course-correct earlier before wasting tokens... and when you sufficiently spec out a coding/software problem, and i mean really document all of the critical nuance, it will successfully satisfy the constraints... the quality is rarely acceptable on first-pass, but it forces me to stay connected to the architecture more than i would be if using a frontier model... i've found this to be a happy middle-ground of productivity and awareness... tested the models on aistudio. despite that the knowledge cut off is march 2026 it still knows nothing about 2025!you can check by asking "list notable world events in 2025, only list unplanned" on aistudio. or you can ask for Charlie Kirk, it also does not know. I tried it multiple time to ensure that I didn't not get routed to older models!> but google has searchirrelevant, without deeper knowledge about cutting edge technologies or latest libraries, all of it suggestions are crap. even you ask it to search it will still use outdated keyword thus only getting outdated information.in other word, what a disaster! update: the knowledge cut off date is "unknown" now.funny because some people downvoted me believed that there is no relation between knowledge cut off date and real world events. that's not how it works! Google desperately needs to make some leadership changes within their Gemini team now that they've been surpassed by 3-5 open weight models and risk loosing frontier status all together in the near future. They don't need to be open weight in the long term; once there's an open-weight Fable-level model with 1M context it'll be pretty much good enough for all coding tasks, no need for new models. At this point, I think google should consider becoming a hyper scaler for anthropic and open ai, and I predict that that is exactly what they do. The model is no longer the most valuable part of the stack.

blocks.html_en:
I wonder how big the Pro model is that Google is using behind the scenes to train these smaller ones.

Going on baseless speculation, the lack of accompanying pro models with these flash releases either means: 1) the model is too big to be economical, 2) google doesn't have the compute to serve the big model, 3) their big model has too many alignment issues to serve to the public.

edit: looks like benchmarks are up on https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flash. It's solidly middle-of-pack. However, if you want to be most fair to flash, look at the intelligence vs time per task and intelligence vs outputspeed benchmarks. This is a very fast model.

edit 2: I use antigravity from time to time and in my experience, 3.5 flash is an underrated model, so long as you know what it's good for. It's very good at frontend (much better than gpt 5.5) and it's fast, so it's a great tool for iteration. I expect 3.6 to be no different.

sentences.en: I wonder how big the Pro model is that Google is using behind the scenes to train these smaller ones.Going on baseless speculation, the lack of accompanying pro models with these flash releases either means: 1) the model is too big to be economical, 2) google doesn't have the compute to serve the big model, 3) their big model has too many alignment issues to serve to the public.edit: looks like benchmarks are up on https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flash. It's solidly middle-of-pack.
sentences.zh: 我想知道谷歌在幕后使用多大的专业模型来训练这些较小的模型。毫无根据的猜测，这些闪存版本缺乏配套的专业模型意味着：1）模型太大而不经济，2）谷歌没有计算能力来服务大模型，3）他们的大模型有太多对齐问题无法向公众提供服务。编辑：看起来基准已经上升https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flash。它确实处于中间位置。

sentences.en: However, if you want to be most fair to flash, look at the intelligence vs time per task and intelligence vs outputspeed benchmarks.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果您想最公平地对待闪存，请查看智能与每个任务的时间以及智能与输出速度基准。

sentences.en: This is a very fast model.edit 2: I use antigravity from time to time and in my experience, 3.5 flash is an underrated model, so long as you know what it's good for.
sentences.zh: 这是一个非常快的模型。编辑2：我时不时地使用反重力，根据我的经验，3.5 flash 是一个被低估的模型，只要你知道它有什么用处。

sentences.en: It's very good at frontend (much better than gpt 5.5) and it's fast, so it's a great tool for iteration.
sentences.zh: 它在前端方面非常出色（比 gpt 5.5 好得多）并且速度很快，因此它是一个很棒的迭代工具。

sentences.en: I expect 3.6 to be no different.
sentences.zh: 我预计 3.6 不会有什么不同。

blocks.html_en:
That and/or the business case isn’t as clear when serving enormous models? You’re constantly stuck in a red queen’s race where your profitability window is increasingly measured in weeks because the Chinese are right behind you.

For small models (which are probably distilled from their big ones) you can serve them economically all the time and not hemorrhage money.

sentences.en: That and/or the business case isn’t as clear when serving enormous models?

blocks.html_en:
> For small models (which are probably distilled from their big ones) you can serve them economically all the time and not hemorrhage money.

For smaller models, you're competing with DeepSeek V4 Flash. (Which I think is a 284B A13B?) Subjectively, this feels about as smart as Sonnet 4.5, give or take. And it costs $0.09/$0.18 on Open Router, compared to $1/$5 for the latest Claude Haiku. See https://openrouter.ai/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash#providers The developer antirez of Redis fame uses this as a local coding model.

DeepSeek did some extremely clever research on hybrid attention to get the prices that low, reducing per-user context cache sizes dramatically.

So, no, when it comes to low-price models, the US models probably can't sustain their current margins there, either.

blocks.html_en:
The Chinese have been right behind OpenAI and Anthropic for ~18 months now.

DeepSeek didn't do to OpenAI and Anthropic what nearly everybody claimed they would.

Every single person on HN that loudly proclaimed the end was nigh for GPT & Co. due to DeepSeek, was wrong. They were humiliatingly wrong, and they'll never own up to it. The reason those people were so very wrong, is the same exact reason the Kimi crowd is wrong now. And it's very obvious that they're wrong, but they have intense emotional blinders on. Their thinking process is hyper emotionalism: they want a certain outcome, regardless of if reality aligns to that or not. They're making emotional wishes about how they want things to turn out, and pretending those magic wishes are grounded in reason.

It takes enormous resources to run something equivalent to GPT 5.6 or Fable. Nobody can or wants to do that outside of very limited situations - if you can just reasonably pay as you go instead. As it turns out, you can just pay as you go with GPT and Fable. Their businesses have gotten radically larger since DeepSeek launched. Get it yet?

Domestic China is the only very large audience for their own models, so long as OpenAI and Anthropic stay top tier.

All the hype online from the forums about Kimi, is worthless: those people hyping it can't even come close to running it locally, which is the fantasy. So why are they hyping it? Why did they hype DeepSeek just the same, and learn nothing from its total failure to actually take down OpenAI and Anthropic? Rhetorical questions with obvious answers.

Kimi poses zero actual threat to OpenAI and Anthropic. Those companies will continue to pile up the subscriptions and API usage. Check out GPT's subscriber base today vs when DeepSeek launched. Get it yet? When Model X launches out of China in a year, we'll have this same conversations all over again, and the hypsters will have learned nothing.

While the Kimi fawning is endless, OpenAI will just keep piling up subscriber counts, and Anthropic will keep piling up API usage. Then OpenAI is going to staple a gigantic ad system onto GPT. China can't compete in the model-as-a-service business globally, for the exact same reason they failed so miserably to compete in search globally.

sentences.zh: HN 上每一个大声宣布 GPT & Co. 的末日即将来临的人。

sentences.en: due to DeepSeek, was wrong.
sentences.zh: 由于DeepSeek，是错误的。

sentences.en: They were humiliatingly wrong, and they'll never own up to it.
sentences.zh: 他们犯了可耻的错误，而且他们永远不会承认这一点。

sentences.en: The reason those people were so very wrong, is the same exact reason the Kimi crowd is wrong now.
sentences.zh: 那些人如此错误的原因，与基米人群现在错误的原因完全相同。

sentences.en: And it's very obvious that they're wrong, but they have intense emotional blinders on.
sentences.zh: 很明显他们错了，但他们有强烈的情感障碍。

sentences.en: Their thinking process is hyper emotionalism: they want a certain outcome, regardless of if reality aligns to that or not.
sentences.zh: 他们的思维过程是高度情绪化的：他们想要某种结果，无论现实是否符合这一结果。

sentences.en: They're making emotional wishes about how they want things to turn out, and pretending those magic wishes are grounded in reason.
sentences.zh: 他们对事情的结果抱有情感上的愿望，并假装这些神奇的愿望是有理性的。

blocks.html_en: It takes enormous resources to run something equivalent to GPT 5.6 or Fable. Nobody can or wants to do that outside of very limited situations - if you can just reasonably pay as you go instead. As it turns out, you can just pay as you go with GPT and Fable. Their businesses have gotten radically larger since DeepSeek launched. Get it yet?

sentences.en: It takes enormous resources to run something equivalent to GPT 5.6 or Fable.
sentences.zh: 运行类似于 GPT 5.6 或 Fable 的东西需要大量资源。

sentences.en: Nobody can or wants to do that outside of very limited situations - if you can just reasonably pay as you go instead.
sentences.zh: 除了非常有限的情况之外，没有人可以或愿意这样做 - 如果你可以合理地按使用付费。

sentences.en: As it turns out, you can just pay as you go with GPT and Fable.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，您可以使用 GPT 和 Fable 按需付费。

sentences.en: Their businesses have gotten radically larger since DeepSeek launched.
sentences.zh: 自 DeepSeek 推出以来，他们的业务规模急剧扩大。

sentences.en: Get it yet?
sentences.zh: 明白了吗？

blocks.html_en: Domestic China is the only very large audience for their own models, so long as OpenAI and Anthropic stay top tier.

sentences.en: Domestic China is the only very large audience for their own models, so long as OpenAI and Anthropic stay top tier.
sentences.zh: 只要 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 保持领先地位，中国国内就是他们自己的模型唯一的大量受众。

blocks.html_en: All the hype online from the forums about Kimi, is worthless: those people hyping it can't even come close to running it locally, which is the fantasy. So why are they hyping it? Why did they hype DeepSeek just the same, and learn nothing from its total failure to actually take down OpenAI and Anthropic? Rhetorical questions with obvious answers.

sentences.en: All the hype online from the forums about Kimi, is worthless: those people hyping it can't even come close to running it locally, which is the fantasy.
sentences.zh: 网上关于 Kimi 的论坛的所有炒作都是毫无价值的：那些炒作它的人甚至无法在本地运行它，这只是幻想。

sentences.en: So why are they hyping it?
sentences.zh: 那他们为什么要炒作呢？

sentences.en: Why did they hype DeepSeek just the same, and learn nothing from its total failure to actually take down OpenAI and Anthropic?
sentences.zh: 为什么他们同样大肆宣传 DeepSeek，却没有从其彻底失败中汲取教训，无法真正击败 OpenAI 和 Anthropic？

sentences.en: Rhetorical questions with obvious answers.
sentences.zh: 带有明显答案的反问句。

blocks.html_en: Kimi poses zero actual threat to OpenAI and Anthropic. Those companies will continue to pile up the subscriptions and API usage. Check out GPT's subscriber base today vs when DeepSeek launched. Get it yet? When Model X launches out of China in a year, we'll have this same conversations all over again, and the hypsters will have learned nothing.

sentences.en: Kimi poses zero actual threat to OpenAI and Anthropic.
sentences.zh: Kimi 对 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的实际威胁为零。

sentences.en: Those companies will continue to pile up the subscriptions and API usage.
sentences.zh: 这些公司将继续增加订阅量和 API 使用量。

sentences.en: Check out GPT's subscriber base today vs when DeepSeek launched.
sentences.zh: 查看 GPT 今天的订阅者基础与 DeepSeek 推出时的情况对比。

sentences.en: Get it yet?
sentences.zh: 明白了吗？

sentences.en: When Model X launches out of China in a year, we'll have this same conversations all over again, and the hypsters will have learned nothing.
sentences.zh: 一年后，当 Model X 在中国推出时，我们将再次进行同样的对话，而狂热者将一无所获。

blocks.html_en: While the Kimi fawning is endless, OpenAI will just keep piling up subscriber counts, and Anthropic will keep piling up API usage. Then OpenAI is going to staple a gigantic ad system onto GPT. China can't compete in the model-as-a-service business globally, for the exact same reason they failed so miserably to compete in search globally.

sentences.en: While the Kimi fawning is endless, OpenAI will just keep piling up subscriber counts, and Anthropic will keep piling up API usage.
sentences.zh: 虽然 Kimi 的奉承是无止境的，但 OpenAI 只会不断增加订阅者数量，而 Anthropic 将不断增加 API 使用量。

sentences.en: Then OpenAI is going to staple a gigantic ad system onto GPT.
sentences.zh: 然后 OpenAI 将把一个巨大的广告系统钉在 GPT 上。

sentences.en: China can't compete in the model-as-a-service business globally, for the exact same reason they failed so miserably to compete in search globally.
sentences.zh: 中国无法在全球模型即服务业务中竞争，其原因与他们在全球搜索竞争中惨败的原因完全相同。

I don't think so. US models are very expensive, and not available in every country. I am not willing to pay $50/1M tokens for writing my pet projects.

blocks.html_en: The AI labs have been subsidising. When they try turn a profit, people will move to the fast followers. The only people that won’t are those that compete on leveraging the very latest models and even then, once spend and scale goes to the cheaper providers, we’ll see deeper research from those providers too. Think “PC compatibles beat IBM, Sun, SGI eventually”.

sentences.en: The AI labs have been subsidising.
sentences.zh: 人工智能实验室一直在提供补贴。

sentences.en: When they try turn a profit, people will move to the fast followers.
sentences.zh: 当他们试图盈利时，人们会转向快速追随者。

sentences.en: The only people that won’t are those that compete on leveraging the very latest models and even then, once spend and scale goes to the cheaper providers, we’ll see deeper research from those providers too.
sentences.zh: 唯一不会的人是那些利用最新模型进行竞争的人，即便如此，一旦支出和规模流向更便宜的提供商，我们也会看到这些提供商进行更深入的研究。

sentences.en: Think “PC compatibles beat IBM, Sun, SGI eventually”.
sentences.zh: 想想“PC 兼容机最终击败了 IBM、Sun、SGI”。

blocks.html_en: Why was this flagged? This is absolutely true, Americans seem to not actually want to use Chinese models at least for coding, maybe for other inference use cases but I haven't seen it. No one I know uses anything but OpenAI and Anthropic even if Chinese models are better or cheaper in many use cases.

sentences.en: No one I know uses anything but OpenAI and Anthropic even if Chinese models are better or cheaper in many use cases.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，没有人使用 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 以外的任何东西，即使中国模型在许多用例中更好或更便宜。

blocks.html_en:
First 6 of the most used models on openrouter currently are Chinese; that's true for code generation and other coding-relevant tasks too when ranked by share of tokens.

https://openrouter.ai/rankings#top-models

Of course openrouter is not representative because most users directly go to the model provider but it still proves your claim is very far from "absolutely true".

blocks.html_en:
Why wouldn't the hyperscalers run these open models since they're much better than OpenAI and Anthropic at operating compute at scale?

I think the reason OpenAI and Anthropic stay ahead in revenues right now is because the models are improving too quickly to reliably compete with them on cost.

But, once model performance reaches a plateau -- they have to at some point, though perhaps years away -- that's when ability to operate compute infrastructure at scale becomes the secret sauce.

The big AI labs are likely safe until models stop improving fast enough to protect them from competition on cost.

This similar pattern has repeated in most technical booms prior to this.

When hard drive technology was improving fast enough that old hard drives were quickly obsolete, IBM could maintain good margins making hard drives. But once hard drives got good enough and advances were slow enough that innovation was not the only factor considered by drive purchasers, commodity hard drives started to take over and IBM had to exit those businesses.

The same is likely to happen once model improvement slows.

sentences.en: Why wouldn't the hyperscalers run these open models since they're much better than OpenAI and Anthropic at operating compute at scale?I think the reason OpenAI and Anthropic stay ahead in revenues right now is because the models are improving too quickly to reliably compete with them on cost.But, once model performance reaches a plateau -- they have to at some point, though perhaps years away -- that's when ability to operate compute infrastructure at scale becomes the secret sauce.The big AI labs are likely safe until models stop improving fast enough to protect them from competition on cost.This similar pattern has repeated in most technical booms prior to this.When hard drive technology was improving fast enough that old hard drives were quickly obsolete, IBM could maintain good margins making hard drives.
sentences.zh: 为什么超大规模企业不运行这些开放模型，因为它们在大规模运行计算方面比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 好得多？我认为 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 目前在收入方面保持领先的原因是因为这些模型改进得太快，无法在成本上与它们可靠地竞争。但是，一旦模型性能达到稳定状态——他们必须在某个时候，尽管可能需要几年时间——那时大规模运行计算基础设施的能力就成为秘密武器。大型人工智能实验室可能是安全的，直到模型停止改进得足够快以保护这种类似的模式在此前的大多数技术繁荣中都曾重复过。当硬盘驱动器技术进步得足够快，旧的硬盘驱动器很快就会被淘汰时，IBM 可以在制造硬盘驱动器方面保持良好的利润。

sentences.en: But once hard drives got good enough and advances were slow enough that innovation was not the only factor considered by drive purchasers, commodity hard drives started to take over and IBM had to exit those businesses.The same is likely to happen once model improvement slows.
sentences.zh: 但是，一旦硬盘驱动器变得足够好，并且进步速度足够慢，以至于创新不再是驱动器购买者考虑的唯一因素，商品硬盘驱动器就开始占据主导地位，IBM 不得不退出这些业务。一旦模型改进速度放缓，同样的情况也可能发生。

blocks.html_en:
> is the same exact reason the Kimi crowd is wrong now. And it's very obvious that they're wrong, but they have intense emotional blinders on.

On the very link on the top comment of this thread, which I repost here:

https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flash

Kimi K3 is ahead of Fable 5 on several benchmarks.

So basically the angle went from "China cannot ever compete" to "China is six months behind" to "China is six weeks behind" to "China is six days behind but that's because they're distilling" and now you're saying "Yup sure, Kimi K3 is ahead on several benchmarks but you cannot host it yourself so this thing will go absolutely nowhere".

I mean: is it not a bit early to draw conclusions? It's been days since a chinese model is ahead of the very best / frontier US model on several benchmarks and you compare it to models who were clearly behind on everything.

Give it some time.

sentences.en: > is the same exact reason the Kimi crowd is wrong now.
sentences.zh: > 这正是 Kimi 人群现在错误的原因。

sentences.en: And it's very obvious that they're wrong, but they have intense emotional blinders on.On the very link on the top comment of this thread, which I repost here:https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flashKimi K3 is ahead of Fable 5 on several benchmarks.So basically the angle went from "China cannot ever compete" to "China is six months behind" to "China is six weeks behind" to "China is six days behind but that's because they're distilling" and now you're saying "Yup sure, Kimi K3 is ahead on several benchmarks but you cannot host it yourself so this thing will go absolutely nowhere".I mean: is it not a bit early to draw conclusions?
sentences.zh: 很明显，他们错了，但他们有强烈的情感蒙蔽。在这个帖子的顶部评论的链接上，我在这里重新发布：https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-6-flashKimi K3 在几个基准上领先于《神鬼寓言 5》。所以基本上，角度从“中国永远无法竞争”到“中国落后六个月”到“中国落后六周”到“中国落后六天，但那是因为他们正在蒸馏”，现在你却说“是的，Kimi K3 在几个基准测试中领先，但你不能自己托管它，所以这件事绝对不会有结果”。我的意思是：现在下结论是不是有点早了？

sentences.en: It's been days since a chinese model is ahead of the very best / frontier US model on several benchmarks and you compare it to models who were clearly behind on everything.Give it some time.
sentences.zh: 自从中国模型在几个基准上领先于最好/前沿的美国模型以来，已经有几天了，你将它与在所有方面都明显落后的模型进行比较。给它一些时间。

blocks.html_en: You’re absolutely right and it’s heartening to see. I maintain a client with ~every provider you can think of and llama.cpp and it was really tiring the last few days to see people laundering other stuff through Kimi and Qwen. They’re not even open yet, the hype was based on their own blog posts, no one’s actually running these locally, the Qwen Max’s have never been open, Kimi’s API was 1/2 the speed the benchmarks was based on, when it was up, and had 60% downtime before they had to stop accepting new accounts, and their EULAs are “your inputs and outputs are ours.” May being clear-eyed benefit us both in the long run.

sentences.en: You’re absolutely right and it’s heartening to see.
sentences.zh: 你是完全正确的，看到这一点真是令人振奋。

sentences.en: I maintain a client with ~every provider you can think of and llama.cpp and it was really tiring the last few days to see people laundering other stuff through Kimi and Qwen.
sentences.zh: 我与你能想到的每一个提供商和 llama.cpp 保持着一个客户，最近几天看到人们通过 Kimi 和 Qwen 洗钱其他东西真的很累。

sentences.en: They’re not even open yet, the hype was based on their own blog posts, no one’s actually running these locally, the Qwen Max’s have never been open, Kimi’s API was 1/2 the speed the benchmarks was based on, when it was up, and had 60% downtime before they had to stop accepting new accounts, and their EULAs are “your inputs and outputs are ours.”
sentences.zh: 他们甚至还没有开放，炒作是基于他们自己的博客文章，没有人真正在本地运行这些，Qwen Max 从未开放过，Kimi 的 API 是基准速度的 1/2，当它启动时，并且在他们不得不停止接受新帐户之前有 60% 的停机时间，他们的 EULA 是“你的输入和输出是我们的”。

sentences.en: May being clear-eyed benefit us both in the long run.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，保持清醒的头脑可能对我们双方都有利。

sentences.zh: 我与你能想到的每一个提供商和 llama.cpp 保持着一个客户，最近几天看到人们通过 Kimi 和 Qwen 洗钱其他东西真的很累。

sentences.en: They’re not even open yet, the hype was based on their own blog posts, no one’s actually running these locally, the Qwen Max’s have never been open, Kimi’s API was 1/2 the speed the benchmarks was based on, when it was up, and had 60% downtime before they had to stop accepting new accounts, and their EULAs are “your inputs and outputs are ours.”
sentences.zh: 他们甚至还没有开放，炒作是基于他们自己的博客文章，没有人真正在本地运行这些，Qwen Max 从未开放过，Kimi 的 API 是基准速度的 1/2，当它启动时，并且在他们不得不停止接受新帐户之前有 60% 的停机时间，他们的 EULA 是“你的输入和输出是我们的”。

sentences.en: May being clear-eyed benefit us both in the long run.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，保持清醒的头脑可能对我们双方都有利。

blocks.html_en: That's the only explanation that makes sense. If it was frontier but cost or compute were limiting factors, they'd release it at an obscene price for the bragging rights. Google doesn't care that much about alignment, and I don't think it's likely to be significantly different than 3.5 anyway. The only reason it would need to be soft-canceled is if it's terrible, and has to end up in a ditch like Llama 4 to avoid shareholder panic.

sentences.en: That's the only explanation that makes sense.
sentences.zh: 这是唯一有意义的解释。

sentences.zh: 如果它是前沿的，但成本或计算是限制因素，他们会为了吹牛的权利而以令人厌恶的价格发布它。

sentences.en: The only reason it would need to be soft-canceled is if it's terrible, and has to end up in a ditch like Llama 4 to avoid shareholder panic.

blocks.html_en:
3.5 pro was clearly a miss. It should have been in prod mid may, not MIA in late July. The brain drain at deep mind is a clear indicator that the people who know the most think that they can’t stay at the frontier.

Antigravity NEEDED to be game-changing. Without the stream of data that Claude, Codex, and Cursor enjoy there is little chance of getting an effective reinforcement learning loop. For the first time in its history, GOOG is at a meaningful data disadvantage, and apparently a cultural one as well.

sentences.en: 3.5 pro was clearly a miss.
sentences.zh: 3.5 pro 显然是一个错过。

sentences.en: It should have been in prod mid may, not MIA in late July.
sentences.zh: 应该是在五月中旬，而不是在七月底的 MIA。

sentences.en: The brain drain at deep mind is a clear indicator that the people who know the most think that they can’t stay at the frontier.Antigravity NEEDED to be game-changing.
sentences.zh: 深层心灵的人才流失是一个明显的指标，表明最了解的人认为他们无法留在前沿。反重力需要改变游戏规则。

sentences.en: Without the stream of data that Claude, Codex, and Cursor enjoy there is little chance of getting an effective reinforcement learning loop.
sentences.zh: 如果没有 Claude、Codex 和 Cursor 所享受的数据流，就几乎没有机会获得有效的强化学习循环。

sentences.en: For the first time in its history, GOOG is at a meaningful data disadvantage, and apparently a cultural one as well.
sentences.zh: GOOG 在其历史上第一次处于数据劣势，而且显然也是文化劣势。

blocks.html_en:
Yes agreed - I wrote this up a while back https://martinalderson.com/posts/whats-going-on-with-gemini/

My view then was they are optimising the models for inference ability on their own hardware AND use cases, which is often speed and time to first token.

They've somehow seemed to end up with terrible compute shortages, which again is surprising given how good Google is at infra deployments AND have their own hardware. From rumors out there they are turning down enterprise deals for Gemini because they don't have the compute.

The problem is they're falling further and further behind on frontier class on coding especially, and since I wrote that article it's got even worse with open weights models undercutting them on price AND intelligence.

sentences.en: Yes agreed - I wrote this up a while back https://martinalderson.com/posts/whats-going-on-with-gemini/My view then was they are optimising the models for inference ability on their own hardware AND use cases, which is often speed and time to first token.They've somehow seemed to end up with terrible compute shortages, which again is surprising given how good Google is at infra deployments AND have their own hardware.
sentences.zh: 是的，同意 - 我不久前写过这篇文章 https://martinalderson.com/posts/whats-going-on-with-gemini/ 我的观点是，他们正在优化自己的硬件和用例上的推理能力模型，这通常是速度和首次标记的时间。不知何故，他们似乎最终出现了严重的计算短缺，考虑到谷歌在基础设施部署方面的出色表现以及拥有自己的硬件，这再次令人惊讶。

sentences.en: From rumors out there they are turning down enterprise deals for Gemini because they don't have the compute.The problem is they're falling further and further behind on frontier class on coding especially, and since I wrote that article it's got even worse with open weights models undercutting them on price AND intelligence.
sentences.zh: 从传言来看，他们拒绝了 Gemini 的企业交易，因为他们没有计算能力。问题是他们在编码方面的前沿水平越来越落后，自从我写那篇文章以来，开放权重模型在价格和情报方面削弱了他们的实力，情况变得更糟。

sentences.zh: 当时我的观点是，他们正在优化模型，以提高自己的硬件和用例的推理能力，这通常是速度和第一个令牌的时间。

blocks.html_en: They've somehow seemed to end up with terrible compute shortages, which again is surprising given how good Google is at infra deployments AND have their own hardware. From rumors out there they are turning down enterprise deals for Gemini because they don't have the compute.

sentences.en: They've somehow seemed to end up with terrible compute shortages, which again is surprising given how good Google is at infra deployments AND have their own hardware.
sentences.zh: 他们似乎以某种方式结束了严重的计算短缺，考虑到谷歌在基础设施部署方面的出色表现以及拥有自己的硬件，这再次令人惊讶。

sentences.en: From rumors out there they are turning down enterprise deals for Gemini because they don't have the compute.
sentences.zh: 据传言称，他们拒绝了 Gemini 的企业交易，因为他们没有计算能力。

blocks.html_en: The problem is they're falling further and further behind on frontier class on coding especially, and since I wrote that article it's got even worse with open weights models undercutting them on price AND intelligence.

sentences.en: The problem is they're falling further and further behind on frontier class on coding especially, and since I wrote that article it's got even worse with open weights models undercutting them on price AND intelligence.
sentences.zh: 问题是，他们在编码方面的前沿水平越来越落后，自从我写那篇文章以来，开放权重模型在价格和情报方面削弱了他们的实力，情况变得更糟。

blocks.html_en:
They include an LLM response with every single Google search, whether it is warranted or not. That scale is, my guess, many orders of magnitude higher than what OpenAI and Anthropic serve. And for Google none of these are paid interactions since their LLMs do not (YET) insert ads into the responses.

So my guess is that Google will continue having compute shortages until the Gemini enshittification starts.

sentences.en: They include an LLM response with every single Google search, whether it is warranted or not.
sentences.zh: 它们在每次谷歌搜索中都包含了法学硕士的回应，无论是否有必要。

sentences.en: That scale is, my guess, many orders of magnitude higher than what OpenAI and Anthropic serve.
sentences.zh: 我猜想，这个规模比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 所服务的规模高出许多数量级。

sentences.en: And for Google none of these are paid interactions since their LLMs do not (YET) insert ads into the responses.So my guess is that Google will continue having compute shortages until the Gemini enshittification starts.
sentences.zh: 对于谷歌来说，这些都不是付费互动，因为他们的法学硕士（还没有）在回复中插入广告。所以我的猜测是，谷歌将继续面临计算短缺，直到双子座enshittification开始。

blocks.html_en:
I don't think so. According to some very basic research there are around 8bn searches a day, or 250bn a month.

Let's assume Google serves AI overviews on every SERP (they don't) and don't cache them (they do, afiak).

And let's assume that each AI overview is 2000 tokens (blended input/output), that's 500T tokens a month.

It's rumoured that anthropic is serving somewhere close to 10Q tokens a month.

Now it may be that AI overviews uses vastly more tokens than that per search, but I doubt it based on speed to render the overview.

My very rough napkin math on this is that maybe AI overviews is consuming 100T tokens/month max (after adjusting for caching and SERPs that don't have them), which would be 1% of Anthropic token volume.

sentences.en: I don't think so.
sentences.zh: 我不这么认为。

sentences.en: According to some very basic research there are around 8bn searches a day, or 250bn a month.Let's assume Google serves AI overviews on every SERP (they don't) and don't cache them (they do, afiak).And let's assume that each AI overview is 2000 tokens (blended input/output), that's 500T tokens a month.It's rumoured that anthropic is serving somewhere close to 10Q tokens a month.Now it may be that AI overviews uses vastly more tokens than that per search, but I doubt it based on speed to render the overview.My very rough napkin math on this is that maybe AI overviews is consuming 100T tokens/month max (after adjusting for caching and SERPs that don't have them), which would be 1% of Anthropic token volume.
sentences.zh: 根据一些非常基础的研究，每天大约有 80 亿次搜索，或者每月 2500 亿次。让我们假设 Google 在每个 SERP 上提供 AI 概述（他们没有）并且不缓存它们（他们确实这样做，afiak）。让我们假设每个 AI 概述有 2000 个令牌（混合输入/输出），即每月 500T 个令牌。有传言说，anthropic 正在接近某个地方提供服务每月 10Q 个代币。现在，AI 概述使用的代币可能比每次搜索多得多，但我对渲染概述的速度表示怀疑。我对此进行了非常粗略的餐巾纸数学计算，也许 AI 概述每月最多消耗 100T 代币（在调整缓存和没有代币的 SERP 后），这将是 Anthropic 代币数量的 1%。

blocks.html_en:
Well I asked the google AI mode thing what it thinks about your comment and it told me this (edited obviously):

"10 Quadrillion tokens a month means: 333 Trillion tokens per day and 3.85 Billion tokens generated/processed every single second, 24/7."

"At an incredibly cheap, subsidized infrastructure cost of $1 per million tokens, serving 10 Quadrillion tokens would cost Anthropic $10 Billion per month ($120 Billion a year) just in inference compute."

It also had this to say about how google's AI overview works: "Google doesn't just feed the LLM your 5-word search query. The system scrapes the top 10–20 web results, feeds thousands of words (tens of thousands of tokens of context) into the model, processes it, and then outputs the result."

Oh, and it does all of that in less than two seconds. Honestly, whatever Google is doing with its infrastructure is so far ahead of everyone else, I can't believe you fell for such an obvious lie.

There are also extremely obvious holes in your comment:

>Let's assume Google serves AI overviews on every SERP (they don't) and don't cache them (they do, afiak).

Try it out for yourself. Add a few random letters or punctuation. They cache nothing.

sentences.en: Well I asked the google AI mode thing what it thinks about your comment and it told me this (edited obviously):"10 Quadrillion tokens a month means: 333 Trillion tokens per day and 3.85 Billion tokens generated/processed every single second, 24/7.""At an incredibly cheap, subsidized infrastructure cost of $1 per million tokens, serving 10 Quadrillion tokens would cost Anthropic $10 Billion per month ($120 Billion a year) just in inference compute."It also had this to say about how google's AI overview works: "Google doesn't just feed the LLM your 5-word search query.
sentences.zh: 好吧，我问谷歌人工智能模式对你的评论有何看法，它告诉我这一点（显然是经过编辑的）：“每月 10 个四万亿代币意味着：每天 333 万亿个代币，每秒生成/处理 38.5 亿个代币，24/7。”“以每百万代币 1 美元的极其便宜的基础设施补贴成本，服务 10 个四万亿代币将花费 Anthropic 每月 100 亿美元（每年 1200 亿美元）仅用于推理计算。”它还谈到了谷歌人工智能概述的工作原理：“谷歌不只是向法学硕士提供 5 个单词的搜索查询。

sentences.en: The system scrapes the top 10–20 web results, feeds thousands of words (tens of thousands of tokens of context) into the model, processes it, and then outputs the result."Oh, and it does all of that in less than two seconds.
sentences.zh: 系统会抓取前 10-20 个网络结果，将数千个单词（数万个上下文标记）输入到模型中，对其进行处理，然后输出结果。“哦，它在不到两秒的时间内完成了所有这些工作。

sentences.en: Honestly, whatever Google is doing with its infrastructure is so far ahead of everyone else, I can't believe you fell for such an obvious lie.There are also extremely obvious holes in your comment:>Let's assume Google serves AI overviews on every SERP (they don't) and don't cache them (they do, afiak).Try it out for yourself.
sentences.zh: 老实说，无论谷歌在其基础设施方面所做的事情都远远领先于其他人，我不敢相信你会相信如此明显的谎言。你的评论中也有极其明显的漏洞：>让我们假设谷歌在每个 SERP 上提供人工智能概述（他们没有）并且不缓存它们（他们确实这样做，afiak）。自己尝试一下。

sentences.en: Add a few random letters or punctuation.
sentences.zh: 添加一些随机字母或标点符号。

sentences.en: They cache nothing.
sentences.zh: 他们什么也不缓存。

It would be a shame if they cannot beat Kimi K3 or Qwen3.8 Max, both of which are claimed to be Fable-like. If that is true, it will be [or would be] the first time a major American lab falls behind a Chinese competitor.

blocks.html_en:
Google can't compete with China, neither can Meta. Only two labs in the US can keep chucking billions at the frontier race. Everyone else has a real business to run.

China can keep up because it's cheaper to run a frontier lab there. They also have more researchers and a stronger cultural inclination for this sort of thing. And I guess the business case in China doesn't have to work as well as it does in the US.

sentences.en: Google can't compete with China, neither can Meta.
sentences.zh: 谷歌无法与中国竞争，Meta 也无法。

sentences.en: Only two labs in the US can keep chucking billions at the frontier race.
sentences.zh: 美国只有两家实验室能够继续在前沿竞赛中投入数十亿美元。

sentences.en: Everyone else has a real business to run.China can keep up because it's cheaper to run a frontier lab there.
sentences.zh: 其他人都有真正的业务要经营。中国可以跟上，因为在那里经营前沿实验室更便宜。

sentences.en: They also have more researchers and a stronger cultural inclination for this sort of thing.
sentences.zh: 他们对这类事情也有更多的研究人员和更强烈的文化倾向。

sentences.en: And I guess the business case in China doesn't have to work as well as it does in the US.
sentences.zh: 而且我认为中国的商业案例并不需要像在美国那样有效。

blocks.html_en:
> China can keep up because it's cheaper to run a frontier lab there

Not sure if this is what you meant, but their training runs are significantly cheaper. This was one of the big shockers from the Deepseek R1 paper. US foreign policy has helped to ensure that the Chinese are compute constrained, so they literally cannot buy the most expensive and powerful training rigs.

This has led to a steady drumbeat of innovations which are not revolutionary on their own but stack together to make things much more efficient.

sentences.en: > China can keep up because it's cheaper to run a frontier lab thereNot sure if this is what you meant, but their training runs are significantly cheaper.
sentences.zh: > 中国可以跟上，因为在那里运营前沿实验室更便宜。不确定这是否是你的意思，但他们的培训费用要便宜得多。

sentences.en: This was one of the big shockers from the Deepseek R1 paper.
sentences.zh: 这是 Deepseek R1 论文中最令人震惊的内容之一。

sentences.en: US foreign policy has helped to ensure that the Chinese are compute constrained, so they literally cannot buy the most expensive and powerful training rigs.This has led to a steady drumbeat of innovations which are not revolutionary on their own but stack together to make things much more efficient.
sentences.zh: 美国的外交政策帮助确保中国人受到计算限制，因此他们实际上无法购买最昂贵和最强大的训练设备。这导致了持续不断的创新，这些创新本身并不是革命性的，但叠加在一起可以使事情变得更加高效。

blocks.html_en:
> ...when is fast a good tradeoff for quality?

When it is cheaper, and the "lower quality" model is adequate for the task at hand.

Plenty of problems have a low(er) skill/intelligence floor, anyone who uses the dual-mode agent paradigm (plan, then act) figures out the second phase can be completed by a less capable model. Even when disregarding costs - speed is important here because the agent can rapidly iterate without human supervision, based on compiler errors, lint and test failures

sentences.en: > ...when is fast a good tradeoff for quality?When it is cheaper, and the "lower quality" model is adequate for the task at hand.Plenty of problems have a low(er) skill/intelligence floor, anyone who uses the dual-mode agent paradigm (plan, then act) figures out the second phase can be completed by a less capable model.
sentences.zh: > ...什么时候快速是质量的良好权衡？当它更便宜，并且“较低质量”模型足以完成手头的任务时。很多问题都具有较低的（呃）技能/智力基础，任何使用双模式代理范式（计划，然后行动）的人都会发现第二阶段可以通过能力较差的模型来完成。

sentences.en: Even when disregarding costs - speed is important here because the agent can rapidly iterate without human supervision, based on compiler errors, lint and test failures
sentences.zh: 即使不考虑成本，速度在这里也很重要，因为代理可以根据编译器错误、lint 和测试失败快速迭代，无需人工监督

blocks.html_en: A coding agent driven by a large LLM can delegate smaller tasks to a faster model. For example searching through the codebase for references, examples, or established patterns. They are treated as tools and don't pollute the main agent's context.

sentences.en: A coding agent driven by a large LLM can delegate smaller tasks to a faster model.
sentences.zh: 由大型法学硕士驱动的编码代理可以将较小的任务委托给更快的模型。

sentences.en: For example searching through the codebase for references, examples, or established patterns.
sentences.zh: 例如，在代码库中搜索参考、示例或已建立的模式。

sentences.en: They are treated as tools and don't pollute the main agent's context.
sentences.zh: 它们被视为工具，不会污染主要代理的上下文。

blocks.html_en:
> focusing on what they can get wins in like speed instead

Speed as a differentiator has always been Google's thing. They (used to?) show the microseconds it took to query & rank web-scale search results. Chrome, notoriously, focused on speed at the expense of resource use. The very many efforts to efficiently speed up Android & its runtime since its inception, and so on...

> their big model underperforms chatgpt 5.6

Possible but TFA claims:

  We have started our most ambitious pre-training run yet, for Gemini 4 ...

sentences.zh: > 专注于他们能在速度等方面取得胜利，而不是速度作为差异化因素一直是谷歌的特色。

sentences.zh: 显示查询和排名网络规模搜索结果所需的微秒数。

sentences.zh: 众所周知，Chrome 专注于速度而牺牲了资源使用。

I've been using 3.5 flash in Android Studio on a Dart/Flutter project, some of it pretty complex and using brand new API's and native code for agentic tool calling in the app.

It used to be that you could find the edges of the training set pretty easily. No longer.

sentences.en: No longer.
sentences.zh: 不再。

blocks.html_en:
I think it's a safe bet that Google seems more interested in making a model that improves Google rather than making a model that improves workers.

Fast, light weight, ok intelligence. Perfect for serving 20B+ prompts per day mostly surrounding banal human things.

OAI and Anthropic's cloud spend can cover the revenue gap, as Google is already capturing a large chunk of those guy's revenue.

sentences.zh: 我认为可以肯定的是，谷歌似乎更感兴趣的是制作一个改进谷歌的模型，而不是制作一个改进工人的模型。速度快，重量轻，智能还可以。

blocks.html_en: AI overview is just a summarization of the top 2-3 results. Of course at Google scale that will still need a ton of compute, but the requirement for generating an overview is many orders of magnitude lower than asking the same question in Gemini.

blocks.html_en:
Microsoft also seems to be working in this space. They recently released this:

https://huggingface.co/microsoft/bitnet-embedding-0.6b

It’s a small multilingual embedding model designed for things like search, RAG, and semantic similarity. It supports a fairly large context window and is designed to run efficiently on a CPU in a GPU starved world.

The interesting part is that it builds on BitNet, using ternary weights of -1, 0, and 1 instead of the usual floating-point weights. That should make indexing and searching large amounts of text much cheaper without giving up too much accuracy.

sentences.en: Microsoft also seems to be working in this space.
sentences.zh: 微软似乎也在这个领域开展工作。

sentences.en: They recently released this:https://huggingface.co/microsoft/bitnet-embedding-0.6bIt’s a small multilingual embedding model designed for things like search, RAG, and semantic similarity.
sentences.zh: 他们最近发布了这个：https://huggingface.co/microsoft/bitnet-embedding-0.6b，这是一个小型多语言嵌入模型，专为搜索、RAG 和语义相似性等而设计。

sentences.en: It supports a fairly large context window and is designed to run efficiently on a CPU in a GPU starved world.The interesting part is that it builds on BitNet, using ternary weights of -1, 0, and 1 instead of the usual floating-point weights.
sentences.zh: 它支持相当大的上下文窗口，并且被设计为在 GPU 匮乏的世界中在 CPU 上高效运行。有趣的是，它基于 BitNet 构建，使用三元权重 -1、0 和 1，而不是通常的浮点权重。

sentences.en: That should make indexing and searching large amounts of text much cheaper without giving up too much accuracy.
sentences.zh: 这将使索引和搜索大量文本的成本大大降低，同时又不会牺牲太多的准确性。

blocks.html_en: Considering how cheap the subscriptions are, it looks like agentic coding is a low margin business. If they can sell you a subscription for the chat, it is profitable, but if you try to use the subscription to its limits, you're probably making them lose money.

blocks.html_en:
> This is a very fast model.

I was already impressed by how fast 3.5 Flash was. But I've never compared it to other models in its class for coding.

Why? Coz models in that class are not very useful to me. Time saved waiting for responses usually just turns into time wasted replying to low quality responses.

Google need to release a Pro model ASAP. I am skeptical of the "maybe they don't have the compute to run it" thing. Anthropic were (probably) in that situation with Mythos and they announced it anyway - that's the obvious play for investor relations as well as hype for your product.

sentences.en: > This is a very fast model.I was already impressed by how fast 3.5 Flash was.
sentences.zh: > 这是一个非常快的模型。3.5 Flash 的速度给我留下了深刻的印象。

sentences.en: But I've never compared it to other models in its class for coding.Why?
sentences.zh: 但我从未将它与同类中的其他模型进行编码比较。为什么？

sentences.en: Coz models in that class are not very useful to me.
sentences.zh: 因为该课程中的模型对我来说不是很有用。

sentences.en: Time saved waiting for responses usually just turns into time wasted replying to low quality responses.Google need to release a Pro model ASAP.
sentences.zh: 等待回复所节省的时间通常只会变成回复低质量回复所浪费的时间。Google 需要尽快发布 Pro 模型。

sentences.en: I am skeptical of the "maybe they don't have the compute to run it" thing.
sentences.zh: 我对“也许他们没有计算能力来运行它”的事情表示怀疑。

sentences.en: Anthropic were (probably) in that situation with Mythos and they announced it anyway - that's the obvious play for investor relations as well as hype for your product.
sentences.zh: Anthropic（可能）与 Mythos 处于这种情况，但无论如何他们还是宣布了这一消息 - 这显然是投资者关系的表现，也是对你的产品的炒作。

blocks.html_en:
It seems like there are some credible rumors that Google is actually winning in terms of actually building models that work and don't lose money- between how they're able to price them, the TPU advantage and their capex advantage (being able to raise debt + just having a lot of cash - well I said not lose money... more like not go bankrupt).

From the outside they look like they're behind in terms of frontier models, but I think they might be the best positioned to not go out of business when the bubble pops.

Also look at the fact that they've been able to deploy AI-assisted search at google scale. It must be another order of magnitude larger (at least) than the model deployments for OpenAI and Anthropic.

Of course unless you're inside Google it's impossible to know for sure.

sentences.en: It seems like there are some credible rumors that Google is actually winning in terms of actually building models that work and don't lose money- between how they're able to price them, the TPU advantage and their capex advantage (being able to raise debt + just having a lot of cash - well I said not lose money...
sentences.zh: 似乎有一些可信的传言称，谷歌实际上在构建有效且不赔钱的模型方面取得了胜利——在他们如何定价、TPU 优势和资本支出优势（能够筹集债务+只是拥有大量现金 - 好吧，我说不赔钱......

sentences.en: more like not go bankrupt).From the outside they look like they're behind in terms of frontier models, but I think they might be the best positioned to not go out of business when the bubble pops.Also look at the fact that they've been able to deploy AI-assisted search at google scale.
sentences.zh: 更像是不破产）。从表面上看，他们在前沿模型方面似乎落后了，但我认为他们可能是在泡沫破裂时不倒闭的最佳人选。还要看看他们已经能够在谷歌规模上部署人工智能辅助搜索这一事实。

sentences.en: It must be another order of magnitude larger (at least) than the model deployments for OpenAI and Anthropic.Of course unless you're inside Google it's impossible to know for sure.
sentences.zh: 它肯定比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的模型部署大（至少）另一个数量级。当然，除非你在 Google 内部，否则不可能确定。

blocks.html_en:
In terms of open models, Gemma 4 beats the pants off everything else to the point that paying for APIs becomes hard to justify. Qwen has the meme-share for coding, but it feels much less well rounded. I have no doubt that Google have both the infrastructure and the expertise to curb stomp everyone else, should they resolve in earnest to do so.

Lest we forget, "Attention is All You Need" came from Google.

sentences.en: In terms of open models, Gemma 4 beats the pants off everything else to the point that paying for APIs becomes hard to justify.
sentences.zh: 就开放模型而言，Gemma 4 击败了其他一切，以至于为 API 付费变得很难证明是合理的。

sentences.en: Qwen has the meme-share for coding, but it feels much less well rounded.
sentences.zh: Qwen 具有用于编码的模因共享，但感觉不太全面。

sentences.en: I have no doubt that Google have both the infrastructure and the expertise to curb stomp everyone else, should they resolve in earnest to do so.Lest we forget, "Attention is All You Need" came from Google.
sentences.zh: 我毫不怀疑谷歌拥有基础设施和专业知识来遏制其他人的践踏，如果他们认真地决心这样做的话。免得我们忘记，“注意力就是你所需要的一切”来自谷歌。

blocks.html_en: In terms of open models, Gemma 4 beats the pants off everything else to the point that paying for APIs becomes hard to justify. Qwen has the meme-share for coding, but it feels much less well rounded. I have no doubt that Google have both the infrastructure and the expertise to curb stomp everyone else, should they resolve in earnest to do so.

blocks.html_en: Interesting, glad to hear. We have gemma4 at work, and I was considering localhosting qwen, but gemma4 is so far behind the Opus and Fable I have at home that I've decided to hold off for another model release.

blocks.html_en: At 20x the parameter count I should hope GLM beats Gemma! But is it 20x better? Expertise is demonstrated, not by making big models, but by making small ones. Bigger isn't better if you can't run it at all.

sentences.en: At 20x the parameter count I should hope GLM beats Gemma!
sentences.zh: 在参数数为 20 倍时，我希望 GLM 能够击败 Gemma！

sentences.en: But is it 20x better?
sentences.zh: 但它好 20 倍吗？

sentences.en: Expertise is demonstrated, not by making big models, but by making small ones.
sentences.zh: 专业知识的展示不是通过制作大模型，而是通过制作小模型。

sentences.en: Bigger isn't better if you can't run it at all.
sentences.zh: 如果你根本无法运行它，那么它并不是越大越好。

blocks.html_en:
It's rumored that Gemini 3.5 flash has a >50% margin, and I'd imagine 3.6 flash is even higher.

I do not think OpenAI or Anthropic are actively chasing margins - though, Anthropic is supposed to be profitable on some form of non-GAAP accounting...

I suspect Google isn't really interested in seeing how far it can get dragged into a race of selling dollars for $0.25, and is more interested to see if it can stay in the race selling $0.50 for a dollar - when everyone else is losing or barely breaking even.

sentences.en: It's rumored that Gemini 3.5 flash has a >50% margin, and I'd imagine 3.6 flash is even higher.I do not think OpenAI or Anthropic are actively chasing margins - though, Anthropic is supposed to be profitable on some form of non-GAAP accounting...I suspect Google isn't really interested in seeing how far it can get dragged into a race of selling dollars for $0.25, and is more interested to see if it can stay in the race selling $0.50 for a dollar - when everyone else is losing or barely breaking even.
sentences.zh: 有传言说 Gemini 3.5 闪存的利润率 >50%，我想 3.6 闪存的利润率甚至更高。我不认为 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 会积极追逐利润率 - 不过，Anthropic 应该在某种形式的非 GAAP 会计上实现盈利……我怀疑 Google 并不真正有兴趣看看它能在 0.25 美元的销售竞赛中拖到什么程度，而更感兴趣的是看看它是否能继续参与销售竞赛1 美元兑 0.50 美元 - 当其他人都亏损或勉强收支平衡时。

blocks.html_en:
It kind of doesn't make sense though, because typically a large org like Google can afford to crush competitors on pricing. They could probably even go toe to toe with chinese model pricing for years without feeling it.

Maybe they don't want to price war with the other labs so they can comfortably maintain healthy margins on selling them compute?

sentences.en: It kind of doesn't make sense though, because typically a large org like Google can afford to crush competitors on pricing.
sentences.zh: 但这有点没有意义，因为通常像谷歌这样的大型组织有能力在定价上压垮竞争对手。

sentences.en: They could probably even go toe to toe with chinese model pricing for years without feeling it.Maybe they don't want to price war with the other labs so they can comfortably maintain healthy margins on selling them compute?
sentences.zh: 他们甚至可能多年来与中国模型的定价针锋相对，却没有感觉到。也许他们不想与其他实验室打价格战，这样他们就可以轻松地在销售计算机时保持健康的利润？

blocks.html_en:
google has to make money. flash is awesome. you can run it free on their infra and the performance and latency is excellent for what you wait and pay for right now, with great perf per watt. every person in the world going to google.com runs it. every query. its far larger than free gpt, localhost qween and what not.

it's their pro that isn't awesome at all. in fact, their pro kinda suck now that everyone else woke up.

sentences.en: google has to make money.
sentences.zh: 谷歌必须赚钱。

sentences.en: flash is awesome.
sentences.zh: 闪光灯太棒了。

sentences.en: you can run it free on their infra and the performance and latency is excellent for what you wait and pay for right now, with great perf per watt.
sentences.zh: 您可以在他们的基础设施上免费运行它，并且性能和延迟非常适合您现在等待和付费的产品，并且每瓦性能很高。

sentences.en: every person in the world going to google.com runs it.
sentences.zh: 世界上每个访问 google.com 的人都会运行它。

sentences.en: every query.
sentences.zh: 每个查询。

sentences.en: its far larger than free gpt, localhost qween and what not.it's their pro that isn't awesome at all.
sentences.zh: 它比免费的 gpt、localhost qween 之类的要大得多。这是他们的专业版，一点也不棒。

sentences.en: in fact, their pro kinda suck now that everyone else woke up.
sentences.zh: 事实上，现在其他人都醒了，他们的职业选手有点糟糕。

blocks.html_en:
That "TPU advantage" might be slowing Google down (though likely not as much as their internal bureaucracy).

Porting CUDA-based research, debugging, and overall experimentation speed is likely slower.

The GPU is still king for training.

sentences.en: That "TPU advantage" might be slowing Google down (though likely not as much as their internal bureaucracy).Porting CUDA-based research, debugging, and overall experimentation speed is likely slower.The GPU is still king for training.
sentences.zh: 这种“TPU 优势”可能会拖慢 Google 的速度（尽管可能不如其内部官僚机构那么严重）。移植基于 CUDA 的研究、调试和整体实验速度可能会变慢。GPU 仍然是训练之王。

blocks.html_en:
"As of October [2025], OpenAI's compute margins reached 70%, up from 52% at the end of 2024 and double the rate in January 2024, [The Information] said, citing a person familiar with the figures."

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-12-21/openai-se...

As for Anthropic, the rumors I remember seeing for their API margins were more like 85-90%, but I don't have a reference at hand for those. But once you know the API is wildly profitable and the subscriptions are roughly break-even and not even a big slice of their income, all of the investment makes a lot more sense.

sentences.en: But once you know the API is wildly profitable and the subscriptions are roughly break-even and not even a big slice of their income, all of the investment makes a lot more sense.
sentences.zh: 但是，一旦您知道 API 利润丰厚，并且订阅量大致达到收支平衡，甚至不占其收入的很大一部分，那么所有投资就变得更有意义了。

blocks.html_en:
That's the profit margin on inference, not overall. Each model does end up being profitable over its lifetime, but the money they're making is being immediately churned into buying more data centers & the training for the next giant model up, so they're not profitable overall at the moment. It's a bit like how Amazon kept churning their profits into more growth instead of taking the profit early.

That said, Anthropic has supposedly crossed over into profitability and made $1 Billion in profit so far this year, in the lead up to their IPO. Being profitable sounds good for launching on the stock market! But as a customer, that's noticeable in the downtime due to lack of compute, and only getting 50% access to Fable.

OpenAI might not be profitable, but they've got so much compute access that they've been able to give their customers full access to Sol, and as a result they've almost doubled their Codex subscriber base in the last two weeks (6 million on July 12, 10 million on July 21 - that would be an extra $1-$10 Billion in Annual Recurring Revenue that they've gained in just these 2 weeks). Doing the unprofitable thing in the short term can result in outsized rewards in the long term.

sentences.en: That's the profit margin on inference, not overall.
sentences.zh: 这是推断的利润率，而不是总体利润率。

sentences.en: Each model does end up being profitable over its lifetime, but the money they're making is being immediately churned into buying more data centers & the training for the next giant model up, so they're not profitable overall at the moment.
sentences.zh: 每个模型最终都会在其生命周期内实现盈利，但他们赚到的钱会立即被用来购买更多的数据中心和下一个巨型模型的培训，因此目前他们总体上还没有盈利。

sentences.en: It's a bit like how Amazon kept churning their profits into more growth instead of taking the profit early.That said, Anthropic has supposedly crossed over into profitability and made $1 Billion in profit so far this year, in the lead up to their IPO.
sentences.zh: 这有点像亚马逊如何不断地将利润转化为更多的增长，而不是提前获利。也就是说，Anthropic 据称已经实现盈利，今年到目前为止，在 IPO 之前已经实现了 10 亿美元的利润。

sentences.en: Being profitable sounds good for launching on the stock market!
sentences.zh: 盈利对于上市来说听起来不错！

sentences.en: But as a customer, that's noticeable in the downtime due to lack of compute, and only getting 50% access to Fable.OpenAI might not be profitable, but they've got so much compute access that they've been able to give their customers full access to Sol, and as a result they've almost doubled their Codex subscriber base in the last two weeks (6 million on July 12, 10 million on July 21 - that would be an extra $1-$10 Billion in Annual Recurring Revenue that they've gained in just these 2 weeks).
sentences.zh: 但作为客户，由于缺乏计算而导致的停机时间很明显，并且只能获得 Fable 的 50% 访问权限。OpenAI 可能无法盈利，但他们拥有如此多的计算访问权限，以至于能够为客户提供对 Sol 的完全访问权限，因此，他们的 Codex 订阅者基数在过去两周内几乎翻了一番（7 月 12 日为 600 万，7 月 21 日为 1000 万 - 这将是每年额外的 1-100 亿美元）他们在这两周内获得的经常性收入）。

sentences.en: Doing the unprofitable thing in the short term can result in outsized rewards in the long term.
sentences.zh: 短期内做无利可图的事情可能会在长期内带来巨大的回报。

blocks.html_en:
On the other hand, the consumer side of the market seems to be less competitive right now.

OpenAI's new Mac app doesn't even have a normal "Chat" option now. OpenAI might be chasing coding and b2b sales more now that they realise very few regular consumers pay for subscriptions.

sentences.en: On the other hand, the consumer side of the market seems to be less competitive right now.OpenAI's new Mac app doesn't even have a normal "Chat" option now.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，消费者方面的市场目前似乎竞争力较弱。OpenAI 的新 Mac 应用程序现在甚至没有正常的“聊天”选项。

sentences.en: OpenAI might be chasing coding and b2b sales more now that they realise very few regular consumers pay for subscriptions.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 可能会更多地追逐编码和 B2B 销售，因为他们意识到很少有普通消费者付费订阅。

On the other hand, the consumer side of the market seems to be less competitive right now.

OpenAI's new Mac app doesn't even have a normal "Chat" option now. OpenAI might be chasing coding and b2b sales more now that they realise very few regular consumers pay for subscriptions.

blocks.html_en: Pre-trains take a huge chunk of your compute offline, incurring both an raw expense (24/7 max power for all training clusters) and an opportunity cost (could have sold excess compute during that time). They also don't come with any great guarantees, as lots of techniques look good on small scale and crumble or plateau once scaled.

sentences.en: Pre-trains take a huge chunk of your compute offline, incurring both an raw expense (24/7 max power for all training clusters) and an opportunity cost (could have sold excess compute during that time).
sentences.zh: 预训练会使您的大量计算离线，从而产生原始费用（所有训练集群的 24/7 最大功率）和机会成本（可能在此期间出售多余的计算）。

sentences.en: They also don't come with any great guarantees, as lots of techniques look good on small scale and crumble or plateau once scaled.
sentences.zh: 它们也没有任何很好的保证，因为许多技术在小规模上看起来不错，但一旦规模扩大就会崩溃或停滞不前。

blocks.html_en: My hunch is Google is trying to integrate a fast and relatively cheap AI across search and every other surface of their product suite. And for that objective, a model that can move faster while being accurate and cheap enough is more important to them than producing a frontier class heavyweight model.

sentences.en: My hunch is Google is trying to integrate a fast and relatively cheap AI across search and every other surface of their product suite.
sentences.zh: 我的预感是，谷歌正在尝试在搜索及其产品套件的每个其他方面集成快速且相对便宜的人工智能。

sentences.en: And for that objective, a model that can move faster while being accurate and cheap enough is more important to them than producing a frontier class heavyweight model.
sentences.zh: 为了实现这一目标，对他们来说，一个能够移动得更快、同时足够准确和便宜的模型比生产前沿级重量级模型更重要。

blocks.html_en: I've found that having good source material (markdown, dependency source, search results for agents) for the models to draw on significantly improves information accuracy. Definitely worth investing in this side of "harness engineering", don't rely on facts burned into weights

sentences.en: I've found that having good source material (markdown, dependency source, search results for agents) for the models to draw on significantly improves information accuracy.
sentences.zh: 我发现，为模型提供良好的源材料（降价、依赖源、代理搜索结果）可以显着提高信息准确性。

sentences.en: Definitely worth investing in this side of "harness engineering", don't rely on facts burned into weights
sentences.zh: 在“线束工程”的这一方面绝对值得投资，不要依赖于重量中的事实

blocks.html_en:
It's a bit disheartening to see no comparison to other models here - and I'm not sure this pushes the curve anywhere. 3.6 flash is more expensive than GLM 5.2 - but seemingly worse, although this post is really light (lite?) on details.

It seemed for a time that Google had finally gotten the ball rolling, but I'm doubting that more and more as time passes. We'll see what happens with 3.5 pro I suppose.

sentences.zh: 看到这里没有与其他模型进行比较，这有点令人沮丧 - 而且我不确定这会将曲线推向任何地方。

sentences.en: We'll see what happens with 3.5 pro I suppose.
sentences.zh: 我想我们会看看 3.5 pro 会发生什么。

blocks.html_en: It’s really surprising. When Apple announced the multi-billion dollar deal with Google to power Apple Intelligence I thought great things were coming. Instead we are getting more and more bad news: delayed Pro models and AI leadership leaving. I wonder if Apple know something the rest of us don’t know or if they are already regretting their decision.

sentences.en: It’s really surprising.
sentences.zh: 真是令人惊讶。

sentences.en: When Apple announced the multi-billion dollar deal with Google to power Apple Intelligence I thought great things were coming.
sentences.zh: 当苹果宣布与谷歌达成数十亿美元的协议以支持苹果智能时，我以为伟大的事情即将到来。

sentences.en: Instead we are getting more and more bad news: delayed Pro models and AI leadership leaving.
sentences.zh: 相反，我们收到了越来越多的坏消息：Pro 模型延迟，AI 领导层离开。

sentences.en: I wonder if Apple know something the rest of us don’t know or if they are already regretting their decision.
sentences.zh: 我想知道苹果是否知道我们其他人不知道的事情，或者他们是否已经后悔自己的决定。

blocks.html_en: Besides Apple apparently making Siri AI model agnostic, the choice to go with Google was almost certainly for practical reasons. Google is a low-risk established player that already has a long work history with Apple. Google also isn't in an existential battle to establish themselves, Gemini still amounts to just another project at Google. There is tangible non-zero risk that either OAI or Anthropic will be gone in 5 years, or will be forced to leave Apple high and dry to save themselves. There is almost no risk Google will be in either such position. And worst case scenario, Google has incredibly deep pockets should Apple pursue a "refund."

sentences.en: Besides Apple apparently making Siri AI model agnostic, the choice to go with Google was almost certainly for practical reasons.
sentences.zh: 除了苹果显然让 Siri 人工智能模型变得不可知之外，选择与谷歌合作几乎肯定是出于实际原因。

sentences.en: Google is a low-risk established player that already has a long work history with Apple.
sentences.zh: 谷歌是一家低风险的老牌企业，与苹果有着悠久的合作历史。

sentences.en: Google also isn't in an existential battle to establish themselves, Gemini still amounts to just another project at Google.
sentences.zh: 谷歌也没有陷入一场生存之战，以确立自己的地位，Gemini 仍然只是谷歌的另一个项目。

sentences.en: There is tangible non-zero risk that either OAI or Anthropic will be gone in 5 years, or will be forced to leave Apple high and dry to save themselves.
sentences.zh: 存在明显的非零风险，即 OAI 或 Anthropic 将在 5 年内消失，或者被迫让苹果陷入困境以自救。

sentences.en: There is almost no risk Google will be in either such position.
sentences.zh: 谷歌几乎不存在处于这两种境地的风险。

sentences.en: And worst case scenario, Google has incredibly deep pockets should Apple pursue a "refund."
sentences.zh: 最坏的情况是，如果苹果寻求“退款”，谷歌将拥有极其雄厚的财力。
