blocks.html_en: What Apple wants out of Google is Siri that runs at 8gb ram and isn’t a horrible embarrassment that feels like a primitive markov chain. Given how good Gemma 4 is, Google can squeeze some serious performance in small models. Whether they can make bleeding edge models is irrelevant to Apple.

sentences.en: What Apple wants out of Google is Siri that runs at 8gb ram and isn’t a horrible embarrassment that feels like a primitive markov chain.
sentences.zh: 苹果希望从谷歌那里得到的是运行在 8GB 内存上的 Siri，而且不会像原始马尔可夫链那样令人尴尬。

sentences.en: Given how good Gemma 4 is, Google can squeeze some serious performance in small models.
sentences.zh: 鉴于 Gemma 4 的出色性能，Google 可以在小型模型中发挥一些重要的性能。

sentences.en: Whether they can make bleeding edge models is irrelevant to Apple.
sentences.zh: 他们能否制造出最先进的型号与苹果无关。

blocks.html_en: As someone on the Apple beta.. the model is almost completely irrelevant to the experience. Apple has gone and done Apple things by nerfing the experience so completely that almost any model in the past year would be fine. I still reach for ChatGPT/Claude/Grok constantly instead of the AI toy that Apple calls the new Siri.

sentences.en: As someone on the Apple beta..
sentences.zh: 作为苹果测试版的某人..

sentences.en: the model is almost completely irrelevant to the experience.
sentences.zh: 该模型几乎与体验完全无关。

sentences.en: Apple has gone and done Apple things by nerfing the experience so completely that almost any model in the past year would be fine.
sentences.zh: 苹果已经通过彻底削弱体验来完成苹果的事情，以至于过去一年中几乎所有型号都可以。

sentences.en: I still reach for ChatGPT/Claude/Grok constantly instead of the AI toy that Apple calls the new Siri.
sentences.zh: 我仍然不断地使用 ChatGPT/Claude/Grok，而不是苹果称之为新 Siri 的人工智能玩具。

blocks.html_en: Apple isn't counting on their model to be a frontier coding and cowork model. Gemini is perfectly fine for the tasks that new Siri is supposed to be doing.

sentences.en: Apple isn't counting on their model to be a frontier coding and cowork model.
sentences.zh: 苹果并不指望他们的模型成为前沿编码和协作模型。

sentences.en: Gemini is perfectly fine for the tasks that new Siri is supposed to be doing.
sentences.zh: Gemini 非常适合新 Siri 应该执行的任务。

blocks.html_en:
I have created various questions/tests and put the models through the same tests.

I record whether the answers are correct, and the generation stats (costs, latencies, tokens used, etc.).

I have no idea why the Gemini models do so well.

I have recently added new tests, whose sole purpose was to find some cases on which Gemini 3 Flash fails (I don't like cherry-picking models or tests, but I also find it strange Gemini Flash models leading in accuracy). I made a more complex coding/tool-usage test, that I expected it to fail, it did fail it once locally in my debug tests, but when I finalized the test and ran the entire testing suite for all models, somehow Gemini 3 Flash still got it right...

Gemini models are REALLY intelligent (and they are actually my favorite model to use via the chat app to ask questions), but they somehow fail in real-word coding tasks where they have to modify files, check results, debug, etc.

My tests harness provides a lot of mock data, and limits the number of actions a model can choose from. I am starting to think that maybe the models are not bad, just that the coding harness are not optimized for those type of models, and Google doesn't really provide their own "Codex".

sentences.en: I have created various questions/tests and put the models through the same tests.I record whether the answers are correct, and the generation stats (costs, latencies, tokens used, etc.).I have no idea why the Gemini models do so well.I have recently added new tests, whose sole purpose was to find some cases on which Gemini 3 Flash fails (I don't like cherry-picking models or tests, but I also find it strange Gemini Flash models leading in accuracy).
sentences.zh: 我创建了各种问题/测试，并对模型进行相同的测试。我记录答案是否正确，以及生成统计数据（成本、延迟、使用的代币等）。我不知道为什么 Gemini 模型做得这么好。我最近添加了新测试，其唯一目的是找到一些 Gemini 3 Flash 失败的案例（我不喜欢挑选模型或测试，但我也发现 Gemini Flash 模型在准确性方面领先很奇怪）。

sentences.en: I made a more complex coding/tool-usage test, that I expected it to fail, it did fail it once locally in my debug tests, but when I finalized the test and ran the entire testing suite for all models, somehow Gemini 3 Flash still got it right...Gemini models are REALLY intelligent (and they are actually my favorite model to use via the chat app to ask questions), but they somehow fail in real-word coding tasks where they have to modify files, check results, debug, etc.My tests harness provides a lot of mock data, and limits the number of actions a model can choose from.
sentences.zh: 我做了一个更复杂的编码/工具使用测试，我预计它会失败，在我的调试测试中它确实在本地失败了一次，但是当我完成测试并为所有模型运行整个测试套件时，不知何故 Gemini 3 Flash 仍然正确...Gemini 模型非常智能（它们实际上是我最喜欢通过聊天应用程序提问的模型），但它们在必须修改文件、检查结果、调试等的实际编码任务中以某种方式失败。我的测试工具提供了很多模拟数据，并限制模型可以选择的操作数量。

sentences.en: I am starting to think that maybe the models are not bad, just that the coding harness are not optimized for those type of models, and Google doesn't really provide their own "Codex".
sentences.zh: 我开始认为也许这些模型还不错，只是编码工具没有针对这些类型的模型进行优化，而且谷歌并没有真正提供自己的“Codex”。

blocks.html_en:
Oh, and I've also added weights to different categories, so Coding and Tool usage categories influence the score more. This done both to better account for how most people are being used, and also to reduce Gemini's dominance in general/domain specific knowledge.

So yes, Gemini models are at the top, even if I actually (not proud of it) tried to make tests that actually favour other coding-focused models.

sentences.en: Oh, and I've also added weights to different categories, so Coding and Tool usage categories influence the score more.
sentences.zh: 哦，我还为不同的类别添加了权重，因此编码和工具使用类别对分数的影响更大。

blocks.html_en:
I don't like to divulge tests, but one of them is a chess puzzle.

> would be great if you could add GPT 5.6 Sol XHigh and Fable 5 High as well

I would like too, but I avoided them for several reasons:

1) Cost - this is a hobby project, those models would cost tens of dollars for each benchmark run, multiply this by tens or hundreds of models and ...

2) Time - the high models are already taking a really long answer to respond (5-10minutes per question). I run each question with 3 repeats (run the same test three times), so it would take 30 minutes per test. If I change my tests, methodology, or add a new test, it would take a really long time to run the benchmark. Also, I like having results immediately when a new model is released, now I can post within 30 minutes of a model's release the benchmark results.

3) High reasoning usually does WORSE on most tests - if you look at the leaderboard, it's sometimes counter-intuitive, but models with high or max reasoning usually do worse than medium and low. This is because the questions are quite targeted/direct, and the models overthink the question and miss the solution. Or the long thinking context makes them perform poorly. The generation tasks (SVGs/HTML animation) are usually better with longer reasoning, but short code fixes, trivia questions, puzzles, etc. are answered by low/med reasoning with more accuracy in general

Also, Fable is borderline un-testable, it refuses to answer many questions, so it scores poorly anyway.

Gemini scores 21/22 because it answers all tests, and it does them correctly, consistently. The only failed test is I think because it miscounted the lines in a file, when responding on which line the bug was in a code snippet.

sentences.en: I run each question with 3 repeats (run the same test three times), so it would take 30 minutes per test.
sentences.zh: 我将每个问题重复 3 次（相同的测试运行 3 次），因此每次测试需要 30 分钟。

sentences.en: If I change my tests, methodology, or add a new test, it would take a really long time to run the benchmark.
sentences.zh: 如果我更改测试、方法或添加新测试，则运行基准测试将需要很长时间。

sentences.en: Also, I like having results immediately when a new model is released, now I can post within 30 minutes of a model's release the benchmark results.3) High reasoning usually does WORSE on most tests - if you look at the leaderboard, it's sometimes counter-intuitive, but models with high or max reasoning usually do worse than medium and low.
sentences.zh: 另外，我喜欢在新模型发布时立即得到结果，现在我可以在模型发布后 30 分钟内发布基准测试结果。3) 在大多数测试中，高推理通常表现更差 - 如果你看一下排行榜，它有时是违反直觉的，但具有高推理或最大推理的模型通常比中推理和低推理的模型表现更差。

sentences.en: This is because the questions are quite targeted/direct, and the models overthink the question and miss the solution.
sentences.zh: 这是因为问题非常有针对性/直接，模型对问题思考过度而错过了解决方案。

sentences.en: Or the long thinking context makes them perform poorly.
sentences.zh: 或者长时间的思考环境使他们表现不佳。

sentences.en: The generation tasks (SVGs/HTML animation) are usually better with longer reasoning, but short code fixes, trivia questions, puzzles, etc. are answered by low/med reasoning with more accuracy in generalAlso, Fable is borderline un-testable, it refuses to answer many questions, so it scores poorly anyway.Gemini scores 21/22 because it answers all tests, and it does them correctly, consistently.
sentences.zh: 生成任务（SVG/HTML 动画）通常在较长的推理中会更好，但短代码修复、琐事问题、谜题等通常可以通过低/中推理来回答，并且通常更准确。而且，《神鬼寓言》处于不可测试的边缘，它拒绝回答许多问题，因此无论如何得分都很差。Gemini 得分 21/22，因为它回答了所有测试，并且正确、一致地完成了这些测试。

sentences.en: The only failed test is I think because it miscounted the lines in a file, when responding on which line the bug was in a code snippet.

This is because the questions are quite targeted/direct, and the models overthink the question and miss the solution. Or the long thinking context makes them perform poorly. The generation tasks (SVGs/HTML animation) are usually better with longer reasoning, but short code fixes, trivia questions, puzzles, etc. are answered by low/med reasoning with more accuracy in general

Also, Fable is borderline un-testable, it refuses to answer many questions, so it scores poorly anyway.

Gemini scores 21/22 because it answers all tests, and it does them correctly, consistently. The only failed test is I think because it miscounted the lines in a file, when responding on which line the bug was in a code snippet.

sentences.en: I don't like to divulge tests, but one of them is a chess puzzle.> would be great if you could add GPT 5.6 Sol XHigh and Fable 5 High as wellI would like too, but I avoided them for several reasons:1) Cost - this is a hobby project, those models would cost tens of dollars for each benchmark run, multiply this by tens or hundreds of models and ...2) Time - the high models are already taking a really long answer to respond (5-10minutes per question).
sentences.zh: 我不想透露测试，但其中一个是国际象棋谜题。> 如果您也可以添加 GPT 5.6 Sol XHigh 和 Fable 5 High 那就太好了我也愿意，但我出于以下几个原因避免了它们：1) 成本 - 这是一个业余爱好项目，这些模型每次基准运行将花费数十美元，乘以数十或数百个模型，然后...2) 时间 - 高模型已经需要很长的答案来响应（每个模型 5-10 分钟）问题）。

blocks.html_en:
You should really provide more on your methodology because as it stands, it really doesn't pass the sniff test. GPT-5.6 Sol on Low beats Fable Medium by 10% and Gemini-3.6 Flash then beats them both? Fable is number 20?

This does not match any lived experience or developer experience.

It'd be helpful to know _what_ you're testing and break that out by dimension. You mention randomly selected questions. How does that work?

With n=22 and binary pass/fail, the 95% confidence interval on a pass rate spans roughly (+-)15-20 percentage points. There's just not enough data ironically, for this leaderboard to mean anything. Ranks #5 through #25 are statistically indistinguishable

blocks.html_en:
Thanks for the feedback, really good points!

There is some short info about the methodology here: https://aibenchy.com/methodology/

> GPT-5.6 Sol on Low beats Fable Medium by 10% > Fable is number 20

Fable loses a lot of points because it often refuses to answer questions. Asking a basic tool-usage challenge, Fable responded with refusal: "This request triggered restrictions on violative cyber content and was blocked under Anthropic's Usage Policy. To learn more, see https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/refusa...." Even in practice, you ask Fable something trivial, and it refuses to respond. I think the score accurately represents how the model is behaving in real-world usage.

> Gemini-3.6 Flash then beats them both Gemini models are the most intelligent overall. The tasks are not coding-only. Gemini excels in general knowledge and domain specific knowledge. Gemini models, even old ones, still top many charts on specific use-cases[0][1]. Depending on how you weigh those cases, the leaderboard order can vary quite drastically, as some models are very strong in some domains and weak in others.

> You mention randomly selected questions. How does that work? Randomly selected, means I have manually created the questions/challenges to span across various domains and agentic surfaces. Questions vary from coding tasks, tool usage, trivia questions, chess puzzles, car-wash-like challenges and more.

> With n=22 and binary pass/fail Each test is run 3 times, so in total we have 66 tasks. Also, apart from correct/wrong answer, the final score also includes the pass rate for each test (how many out of three attempts), how good the reasoning is (they have a hidden reasoning score where available) and other small factors. Also, some tests in some categories involve a series of tasks/requirements (i.e. implement this function, call it, do some processing on the result, combine the result with some built-in knowledge data, etc.).

I do agree that 22 tests isn't that much, and I'm slowly adding more, but even without the leaderboard part, the comparison feature is what's I think is most useful. You can see for the exact same tasks, which models do better, which do it faster, which cost less, etc.

> _what_ you're testing and break that out by dimension There is a category breakdown for the test results, so you can see and which sort of tasks models fail.

Everything aside, when you manually ask a model to test its capabilities, I don't think it takes many questions to realise how good/bad that model is. Sometimes one prompt is enough, you ask it to do something, and see how it reasons about it, how fast it does it, how efficient the steps are and how good the result is. Yes, the performance may vary across tasks, but I'm pretty sure if you did a blind test with a chatbot, you could easily realise how good the model is in just a few questions/tasks.

I think no benchmark is perfect, mine is far from it, but it's simply another different, independent data-point. Apart from that, I made this for myself, and I'm using it myself. I don't trust that all popular benchmarks are not in the training data, and I think many benchmark the wrong things which don't correlate to how I use the models day-to-day myself. I just made the results publicly available, in case any one else benefits from it. I've probably spent thousands in LLM costs, and probably more than 100 hour building this, without benefiting in any way from it (outside of the joy of building it and me using it personally to compare models); as long as models cost stays reasonable, I'll keep building it and test new models as soon as they are released.

[0]: https://x.com/browser_use/status/2079602472516264010/photo/1 [1]: https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmmu-pro#mmmu-pro-...

sentences.zh: 有时一个提示就足够了，你要求它做某件事，看看它是如何推理的，它做的速度有多快，步骤有多高效以及结果有多好。

sentences.en: Yes, the performance may vary across tasks, but I'm pretty sure if you did a blind test with a chatbot, you could easily realise how good the model is in just a few questions/tasks.I think no benchmark is perfect, mine is far from it, but it's simply another different, independent data-point.
sentences.zh: 是的，不同任务的性能可能会有所不同，但我很确定，如果您使用聊天机器人进行了盲测，您可以轻松地意识到该模型在几个问题/任务中的表现有多好。我认为没有哪个基准是完美的，我的基准远非完美，但这只是另一个不同的独立数据点。

sentences.en: Apart from that, I made this for myself, and I'm using it myself.
sentences.zh: 除此之外，这是我为自己制作的，并且我自己正在使用它。

sentences.en: I don't trust that all popular benchmarks are not in the training data, and I think many benchmark the wrong things which don't correlate to how I use the models day-to-day myself.
sentences.zh: 我不相信所有流行的基准测试都不在训练数据中，而且我认为许多基准测试都是错误的，这与我自己日常使用模型的方式无关。

sentences.en: I just made the results publicly available, in case any one else benefits from it.
sentences.zh: 我刚刚将结果公开，以防其他人从中受益。

sentences.en: I've probably spent thousands in LLM costs, and probably more than 100 hour building this, without benefiting in any way from it (outside of the joy of building it and me using it personally to compare models); as long as models cost stays reasonable, I'll keep building it and test new models as soon as they are released.[0]: https://x.com/browser_use/status/2079602472516264010/photo/1 [1]: https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmmu-pro#mmmu-pro-...
sentences.zh: 我可能花费了数千美元的 LLM 成本，并且可能花费了 100 多个小时来构建这个，但没有从中获得任何好处（除了构建它的乐趣以及我个人使用它来比较模型之外）；只要模型成本保持合理，我就会继续构建它并在新模型发布后立即对其进行测试。[0]：https://x.com/browser_use/status/2079602472516264010/photo/1 [1]：https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmmu-pro#mmmu-pro-...

blocks.html_en:
Thanks for the feedback, really good points!

There is some short info about the methodology here: https://aibenchy.com/methodology/

> GPT-5.6 Sol on Low beats Fable Medium by 10% > Fable is number 20

Fable loses a lot of points because it often refuses to answer questions. Asking a basic tool-usage challenge, Fable responded with refusal: "This request triggered restrictions on violative cyber content and was blocked under Anthropic's Usage Policy. To learn more, see https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/refusa...." Even in practice, you ask Fable something trivial, and it refuses to respond. I think the score accurately represents how the model is behaving in real-world usage.

> Gemini-3.6 Flash then beats them both Gemini models are the most intelligent overall. The tasks are not coding-only. Gemini excels in general knowledge and domain specific knowledge. Gemini models, even old ones, still top many charts on specific use-cases[0][1]. Depending on how you weigh those cases, the leaderboard order can vary quite drastically, as some models are very strong in some domains and weak in others.

> You mention randomly selected questions. How does that work? Randomly selected, means I have manually created the questions/challenges to span across various domains and agentic surfaces. Questions vary from coding tasks, tool usage, trivia questions, chess puzzles, car-wash-like challenges and more.

> With n=22 and binary pass/fail Each test is run 3 times, so in total we have 66 tasks. Also, apart from correct/wrong answer, the final score also includes the pass rate for each test (how many out of three attempts), how good the reasoning is (they have a hidden reasoning score where available) and other small factors. Also, some tests in some categories involve a series of tasks/requirements (i.e. implement this function, call it, do some processing on the result, combine the result with some built-in knowledge data, etc.).

I do agree that 22 tests isn't that much, and I'm slowly adding more, but even without the leaderboard part, the comparison feature is what's I think is most useful. You can see for the exact same tasks, which models do better, which do it faster, which cost less, etc.

> _what_ you're testing and break that out by dimension There is a category breakdown for the test results, so you can see and which sort of tasks models fail.

Everything aside, when you manually ask a model to test its capabilities, I don't think it takes many questions to realise how good/bad that model is. Sometimes one prompt is enough, you ask it to do something, and see how it reasons about it, how fast it does it, how efficient the steps are and how good the result is. Yes, the performance may vary across tasks, but I'm pretty sure if you did a blind test with a chatbot, you could easily realise how good the model is in just a few questions/tasks.

I think no benchmark is perfect, mine is far from it, but it's simply another different, independent data-point. Apart from that, I made this for myself, and I'm using it myself. I don't trust that all popular benchmarks are not in the training data, and I think many benchmark the wrong things which don't correlate to how I use the models day-to-day myself. I just made the results publicly available, in case any one else benefits from it. I've probably spent thousands in LLM costs, and probably more than 100 hour building this, without benefiting in any way from it (outside of the joy of building it and me using it personally to compare models); as long as models cost stays reasonable, I'll keep building it and test new models as soon as they are released.

[0]: https://x.com/browser_use/status/2079602472516264010/photo/1 [1]: https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmmu-pro#mmmu-pro-...

sentences.en: I've probably spent thousands in LLM costs, and probably more than 100 hour building this, without benefiting in any way from it (outside of the joy of building it and me using it personally to compare models); as long as models cost stays reasonable, I'll keep building it and test new models as soon as they are released.
sentences.zh: 我可能花费了数千美元的 LLM 成本，并且可能花费了 100 多个小时来构建这个，但没有从中获得任何好处（除了构建它的乐趣以及我个人使用它来比较模型之外）；只要模型成本保持合理，我就会继续构建它并在新模型发布后立即对其进行测试。

blocks.html_en: But to run the entire benchmark it cost $727 with Gemini 3.6 Flash and $925 with GLM-5.2, $198 (21.4%) less. I tend to look at the cost to run the whole index rather than the weighted average cost per task.

sentences.zh: 但要运行整个基准测试，Gemini 3.6 Flash 的成本为 727 美元，GLM-5.2 的成本为 925 美元，少了 198 美元（21.4%）。

sentences.en: I tend to look at the cost to run the whole index rather than the weighted average cost per task.
sentences.zh: 我倾向于查看运行整个索引的成本，而不是每个任务的加权平均成本。

blocks.html_en: The one thing I've found google's models to be the best at is proofreading text in non-english languages. Probably because I imagine they have the most training data for it as Google probably has the most complete archive of the internet.

blocks.html_en:
Google somehow managed to snatch defeat from the jaws of success with their AI products.

They literally forced me and my company out of Antigravity by phasing out AI Ultra subscription without any proper product follow-up. Antigravity IDE cannot even have poweruser subscriptions now from Google Workspace an Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform cannot be attached to Antigravity IDE.

Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform has an incredibly abysmal setup process, and if I want to limit spending per-user I have to create projects per user. The fact that you cannot activate Anthropic models on it if the billing still has free credits is almost a joke.

I was a big proponent of Google and Gemini, but they left us reeling with their abrupt product decisions. Forced us to buy $200 subscriptions directly from Anthropic/OpenAI.

sentences.en: Google somehow managed to snatch defeat from the jaws of success with their AI products.They literally forced me and my company out of Antigravity by phasing out AI Ultra subscription without any proper product follow-up.
sentences.zh: 谷歌以某种方式成功地通过他们的人工智能产品从成功的陷阱中夺回了失败。他们通过逐步取消人工智能超级订阅而没有任何适当的产品后续行动，实际上迫使我和我的公司退出了反重力。

sentences.en: Antigravity IDE cannot even have poweruser subscriptions now from Google Workspace an Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform cannot be attached to Antigravity IDE.Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform has an incredibly abysmal setup process, and if I want to limit spending per-user I have to create projects per user.
sentences.zh: Antigravity IDE 现在甚至无法从 Google Workspace 获得高级用户订阅，Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform 无法附加到 Antigravity IDE。Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform 的设置过程非常糟糕，如果我想限制每个用户的支出，我必须为每个用户创建项目。

sentences.en: The fact that you cannot activate Anthropic models on it if the billing still has free credits is almost a joke.I was a big proponent of Google and Gemini, but they left us reeling with their abrupt product decisions.
sentences.zh: 事实上，如果账单仍然有免费积分，你就无法激活人机模型，这几乎是一个笑话。我是谷歌和双子座的大力支持者，但他们让我们对他们突然的产品决定感到震惊。

sentences.en: Forced us to buy $200 subscriptions directly from Anthropic/OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 迫使我们直接从 Anthropic/OpenAI 购买 200 美元的订阅。

It's pretty weird that I have access to more powerful tools when using my personal Google account compared to my corporate Google Workspace account.

blocks.html_en:
It's literally the meme of the MS org chart pointing guns at each other.

The GCP team wants their slice, the other team wants some otjer slice, and so on. Everyone wants some crap for their promotion package.

It's no wonder Meta has shit the bed even worse.

It's also why Google still releases actually decent, useful models despite the product being such a hilarious mess. A lot of the time Gemini models have actually been better as production LLMs as part of LLM-based production applications than OpenAI and Anthropic models when it comes to the complete cost:quality:latency:adherence picture. And they still are. We have products in prod that use Gemini because they're better than any other model at the specific task. But we wouldn't dare use it for anything coding related, or even just as productivity tool to rely on, because as a consumer product it's a joke.

sentences.en: It's literally the meme of the MS org chart pointing guns at each other.The GCP team wants their slice, the other team wants some otjer slice, and so on.
sentences.zh: 这实际上是 MS 组织结构图上用枪指着对方的模因。GCP 团队想要他们的份额，另一个团队想要一些 otjer 份额，等等。

sentences.en: Everyone wants some crap for their promotion package.It's no wonder Meta has shit the bed even worse.It's also why Google still releases actually decent, useful models despite the product being such a hilarious mess.
sentences.zh: 每个人都希望他们的促销套餐有一些废话。难怪 Meta 的表现更糟糕。这也是为什么谷歌仍然发布真正不错、有用的模型，尽管产品如此混乱。

sentences.en: A lot of the time Gemini models have actually been better as production LLMs as part of LLM-based production applications than OpenAI and Anthropic models when it comes to the complete cost:quality:latency:adherence picture.
sentences.zh: 很多时候，就完整的成本：质量：延迟：依从性而言，Gemini 模型作为生产 LLM 作为基于 LLM 的生产应用程序的一部分，实际上比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 模型更好。

sentences.en: And they still are.
sentences.zh: 现在仍然如此。

sentences.en: We have products in prod that use Gemini because they're better than any other model at the specific task.
sentences.zh: 我们的产品使用 Gemini，因为它们在特定任务上比任何其他模型都更好。

sentences.en: But we wouldn't dare use it for anything coding related, or even just as productivity tool to rely on, because as a consumer product it's a joke.
sentences.zh: 但我们不敢将它用于任何与编码相关的事情，甚至不敢将其用作依赖的生产力工具，因为作为消费产品，这是一个笑话。

blocks.html_en:
It's literally the meme of the MS org chart pointing guns at each other.

The GCP team wants their slice, the other team wants some otjer slice, and so on. Everyone wants some crap for their promotion package.

It's no wonder Meta has shit the bed even worse.

It's also why Google still releases actually decent, useful models despite the product being such a hilarious mess. A lot of the time Gemini models have actually been better as production LLMs as part of LLM-based production applications than OpenAI and Anthropic models when it comes to the complete cost:quality:latency:adherence picture. And they still are. We have products in prod that use Gemini because they're better than any other model at the specific task. But we wouldn't dare use it for anything coding related, or even just as productivity tool to rely on, because as a consumer product it's a joke.

sentences.zh: 这实际上是 MS 组织结构图上用枪指着对方的模因。GCP 团队想要他们的份额，另一个团队想要一些 otjer 份额，等等。

sentences.en: Everyone wants some crap for their promotion package.It's no wonder Meta has shit the bed even worse.It's also why Google still releases actually decent, useful models despite the product being such a hilarious mess.
sentences.zh: 每个人都希望他们的促销套餐有一些废话。难怪 Meta 的表现更糟糕。这也是为什么谷歌仍然发布真正不错、有用的模型，尽管产品如此混乱。

> The GCP team wants their slice, the other team wants some otjer slice, and so on. Everyone wants some crap for their promotion package.

I got a Google One plan for Gemini, but it came bundled with YT Premium lite, and that somehow made it impossible to renew YT Premium for 30 days. I suspect different teams stealing customers from each other.

blocks.html_en:
Google also gave away 1 year Gemini plans with Pixel phones that either did not work at all for existing Google One users or messed up subscriptions by downgrading your account to worse plan, or making your existing paid time shorter if you been on cheaper plan or recently changee countries. Etc.

Like when you try to give Google money they try to squeeze you as much as possible.

At the same time you can get 5 time more limits for free just by registering 10 free Google accounts.

Google subscriptions are one big mess.

sentences.en: Google also gave away 1 year Gemini plans with Pixel phones that either did not work at all for existing Google One users or messed up subscriptions by downgrading your account to worse plan, or making your existing paid time shorter if you been on cheaper plan or recently changee countries.
sentences.zh: 谷歌还赠送了 Pixel 手机的 1 年 Gemini 计划，这些计划要么对现有的 Google One 用户根本不起作用，要么通过将你的帐户降级到更糟糕的计划来搞乱订阅，或者如果你使用的是更便宜的计划或最近改变了国家，则缩短你现有的付费时间。

sentences.en: Etc.Like when you try to give Google money they try to squeeze you as much as possible.At the same time you can get 5 time more limits for free just by registering 10 free Google accounts.Google subscriptions are one big mess.
sentences.zh: 等等。就像当你试图给谷歌钱时，他们会尽可能地压榨你。同时，你只需注册 10 个免费的谷歌帐户，就可以免费获得 5 倍的限制。谷歌订阅是一团糟。

blocks.html_en:
Exactly my experience. I'm building an AI document-extraction platform, so I had to benchmark a bunch of models — on the cost:quality:latency:adherence picture, flash wins hands down for structured extraction. (Caveat: I've only tested the three US labs and Mistral.). So like u said, totally viable in prod for a relatively static tool. Didn't build the tool suite with gemini, but if you use service mode it currently mainly runs on flash.

Haven't done any serious coding work with the flash models though — but I'm seeing more and more HN comments from people who seem to have picked it up for that in the last couple of months.

sentences.en: Exactly my experience.
sentences.zh: 这正是我的经历。

sentences.en: I'm building an AI document-extraction platform, so I had to benchmark a bunch of models — on the cost:quality:latency:adherence picture, flash wins hands down for structured extraction.
sentences.zh: 我正在构建一个人工智能文档提取平台，因此我必须对一堆模型进行基准测试 - 在成本：质量：延迟：依从性方面，闪存在结构化提取方面轻而易举地获胜。

sentences.en: (Caveat: I've only tested the three US labs and Mistral.).
sentences.zh: （警告：我只测试了三个美国实验室和米斯特拉尔。）。

sentences.en: So like u said, totally viable in prod for a relatively static tool.
sentences.zh: 就像你说的，对于一个相对静态的工具来说，在产品中完全可行。

sentences.en: Didn't build the tool suite with gemini, but if you use service mode it currently mainly runs on flash.Haven't done any serious coding work with the flash models though — but I'm seeing more and more HN comments from people who seem to have picked it up for that in the last couple of months.
sentences.zh: 没有使用 Gemini 构建工具套件，但如果您使用服务模式，它目前主要在闪存上运行。虽然还没有对闪存模型进行任何认真的编码工作 - 但我看到越来越多的 HN 评论来自似乎在过去几个月中接受它的人。

blocks.html_en:
As someone who's been using Workspace as a personal email account for over a decade this has been such a struggle forever. Just lots of odd limitations to feature sets all over the place.

When they swapped Google Assistant for Gemini as the default voice provider in Android Auto it was so annoying. My wife's non-work space account can get Gemini to do the normal things like play music and what not, but my Workspace one can't do much of anything at all. I can talk about nearly any random topic with it, but getting it to change the playlist, nah, can't help you there.

It's no surprise to me to see them fumble actually supporting a lot of the consumer features of Gemini into Workspace.

sentences.en: As someone who's been using Workspace as a personal email account for over a decade this has been such a struggle forever.
sentences.zh: 对于十多年来一直使用 Workspace 作为个人电子邮件帐户的人来说，这一直是一场斗争。

sentences.en: Just lots of odd limitations to feature sets all over the place.When they swapped Google Assistant for Gemini as the default voice provider in Android Auto it was so annoying.
sentences.zh: 只是到处都有很多奇怪的功能设置限制。当他们将 Google Assistant 换成 Gemini 作为 Android Auto 中的默认语音提供程序时，这真是太烦人了。

sentences.en: My wife's non-work space account can get Gemini to do the normal things like play music and what not, but my Workspace one can't do much of anything at all.
sentences.zh: 我妻子的非工作空间帐户可以让 Gemini 做一些正常的事情，比如播放音乐之类的，但我的工作空间帐户根本不能做任何事情。

sentences.en: I can talk about nearly any random topic with it, but getting it to change the playlist, nah, can't help you there.It's no surprise to me to see them fumble actually supporting a lot of the consumer features of Gemini into Workspace.
sentences.zh: 我可以用它谈论几乎任何随机主题，但让它改变播放列表，不，不能帮助你。看到他们摸索着将 Gemini 的许多消费者功能支持到 Workspace 中，我并不感到惊讶。

sentences.en: My wife's non-work space account can get Gemini to do the normal things like play music and what not, but my Workspace one can't do much of anything at all.
sentences.zh: 我妻子的非工作空间帐户可以让 Gemini 做一些正常的事情，比如播放音乐之类的，但我的工作空间帐户根本不能做任何事情。

sentences.en: I can talk about nearly any random topic with it, but getting it to change the playlist, nah, can't help you there.
sentences.zh: 我可以用它谈论几乎任何随机话题，但让它改变播放列表，不，不能帮助你。

blocks.html_en: It's no surprise to me to see them fumble actually supporting a lot of the consumer features of Gemini into Workspace.

sentences.en: It's no surprise to me to see them fumble actually supporting a lot of the consumer features of Gemini into Workspace.
sentences.zh: 看到他们笨手笨脚地将 Gemini 的许多消费者功能支持到 Workspace 中，我并不感到惊讶。

blocks.html_en: I'm in the same situation. But I was shocked discovering it goes both ways: many new Gemini functionalities are only accessible using a consumer account instead of a Workspace account. Also, Gemini is now the only major AI assistant with no support for MCP connectors. Instead of adding this to the core product, like ChatGPT and Claude did, somebody at Google decided that it was smarter to add this fundamental feature to a new product instead: for enterprises this is Gemini Enterprise (which is a product completely different from the Gemini app); for consumers this the new Gemini Spark agent (meaning that you can use MCP within Spark but not within a "non-agentic" chat)... It's clear to me this a symptom of Google shipping their org chart, which is a disaster from a product perspective.

sentences.en: I'm in the same situation.
sentences.zh: 我也有同样的情况。

sentences.en: But I was shocked discovering it goes both ways: many new Gemini functionalities are only accessible using a consumer account instead of a Workspace account.
sentences.zh: 但我惊讶地发现它是双向的：许多新的 Gemini 功能只能使用消费者帐户而不是工作区帐户访问。

sentences.en: Also, Gemini is now the only major AI assistant with no support for MCP connectors.
sentences.zh: 此外，Gemini 是目前唯一不支持 MCP 连接器的主要 AI 助手。

sentences.en: Instead of adding this to the core product, like ChatGPT and Claude did, somebody at Google decided that it was smarter to add this fundamental feature to a new product instead: for enterprises this is Gemini Enterprise (which is a product completely different from the Gemini app); for consumers this the new Gemini Spark agent (meaning that you can use MCP within Spark but not within a "non-agentic" chat)...
sentences.zh: 谷歌的某人没有像 ChatGPT 和 Claude 那样将其添加到核心产品中，而是认为将这一基本功能添加到新产品中更为明智：对于企业来说，这是 Gemini Enterprise（这是一个与 Gemini 应用程序完全不同的产品）；对于企业来说，这是 Gemini Enterprise（这是一个与 Gemini 应用程序完全不同的产品）；对于消费者来说，这是新的 Gemini Spark 代理（这意味着您可以在 Spark 中使用 MCP，但不能在“非代理”聊天中使用）...

sentences.en: It's clear to me this a symptom of Google shipping their org chart, which is a disaster from a product perspective.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，很明显这是谷歌发布其组织结构图的症状，从产品的角度来看这是一场灾难。

blocks.html_en:
I currently have a free trial AI Pro subscription that will run out next month.

If it weren't for the $10 GCP credit, I'd straight away cancel it. I don't see enough value in Gemini to justify the $20 subscription.

sentences.en: I currently have a free trial AI Pro subscription that will run out next month.If it weren't for the $10 GCP credit, I'd straight away cancel it.
sentences.zh: 我目前有一个免费试用版 AI Pro 订阅，该订阅将于下个月到期。如果不是 10 美元的 GCP 积分，我会立即取消它。

sentences.en: I don't see enough value in Gemini to justify the $20 subscription.
sentences.zh: 我认为 Gemini 没有足够的价值来证明 20 美元的订阅是合理的。

They might have success if they tried maybe a 12-15 dollar tier.

sentences.zh: 两家公司都需要更强大的自上而下的产品思维。

blocks.html_en: This drove me bonkers. You can enable play music (etc) in Android Auto for workspace accounts by enabling apps in Gemini. From memory (looking at the settings now, not 100% sure of the magic steps required), but go to admin.google.com, go to 'generative ai', 'gemini app', and 'apps settings', then turn on 'other Google apps'. This lets you play music (and other things) in Android Auto.

sentences.en: This drove me bonkers.
sentences.zh: 这让我发疯。

sentences.en: You can enable play music (etc) in Android Auto for workspace accounts by enabling apps in Gemini.
sentences.zh: 您可以通过在 Gemini 中启用应用程序来在 Android Auto 中为工作区帐户启用播放音乐（等）。

sentences.en: From memory (looking at the settings now, not 100% sure of the magic steps required), but go to admin.google.com, go to 'generative ai', 'gemini app', and 'apps settings', then turn on 'other Google apps'.
sentences.zh: 根据记忆（现在查看设置，不能 100% 确定所需的神奇步骤），但转到 admin.google.com，转到“生成人工智能”、“双子座应用程序”和“应用程序设置”，然后打开“其他 Google 应用程序”。

sentences.en: This lets you play music (and other things) in Android Auto.
sentences.zh: 这可以让您在 Android Auto 中播放音乐（和其他内容）。

blocks.html_en:
It's absolute insanity. They have all the resources to have been able to lead from the front with this new technology. They have the products and users to integrate this technology into people's already existing lives. But they keep fumbling.

They're not even benchmarking against other models now, just against themselves - which tells you everything you need to know.

sentences.zh: 这绝对是疯狂。

sentences.zh: 他们拥有将这项技术融入人们现有生活的产品和用户。

sentences.en: But they keep fumbling.They're not even benchmarking against other models now, just against themselves - which tells you everything you need to know.

blocks.html_en:
What does "leading from the front" get them?

There is absolutely no loyalty when it comes to coding. Nothing could be more common than people threating to jump ship whenever another frontier or open source model comes.

Google is clearly able to keep growing their free and consumer and small business use cases. Unlike corporate coding, we actually have evidence that solo and small businesses can actually see productivity gains.

Anthropic and OpenAI need to stay dancing like mad, because it's their source revenue which underpins their investments.

Why does Google need to shove something at the top at the same desperate cadence? Other than "recursive self improvement leads to AGI" it seems perfectly fine if they push out something dramatically better every year and half.

sentences.en: What does "leading from the front" get them?There is absolutely no loyalty when it comes to coding.
sentences.zh: “身先士卒”给他们带来了什么？在编码方面绝对没有忠诚度。

sentences.en: Nothing could be more common than people threating to jump ship whenever another frontier or open source model comes.Google is clearly able to keep growing their free and consumer and small business use cases.
sentences.zh: 每当另一个前沿或开源模型出现时，没有什么比人们威胁要跳槽更常见的了。谷歌显然能够继续发展其免费、消费者和小型企业用例。

sentences.en: Unlike corporate coding, we actually have evidence that solo and small businesses can actually see productivity gains.Anthropic and OpenAI need to stay dancing like mad, because it's their source revenue which underpins their investments.Why does Google need to shove something at the top at the same desperate cadence?
sentences.zh: 与企业编码不同，我们实际上有证据表明，个体企业和小型企业实际上可以看到生产力的提高。Anthropic 和 OpenAI 需要疯狂地跳舞，因为这是他们的投资来源。为什么谷歌需要以同样绝望的节奏将某些东西推到最高层？

sentences.en: Other than "recursive self improvement leads to AGI" it seems perfectly fine if they push out something dramatically better every year and half.
sentences.zh: 除了“递归自我改进导致 AGI”之外，如果他们每年半推出一些明显更好的东西，那似乎完全没问题。

This is still very early days. Who is "on top" has flipped back and forth many times already. The next frontier model release (from whomever) will change things again.

blocks.html_en:
I don't think it's that early tbh, agentic coding has ~90% adoption in the US.

Claude Code has largely won individual developer mindshare and has been on top ever since it came out. The benchmarks change, but almost nobody opts to use anything other than Claude IME when I ask them. Enterprise is more competitive since they care about costs and other things, but developers leaning towards Claude puts a thumb on the scales there.

The product doesn't have much lock in, so it is possible to dislodge Claude, and Anthropic could (and some may argue is likely to) just shoot themselves in the foot again and again and again, but Google has never been particularly good at enterprise sales, and they have never actually been at the frontier of intelligence.

I think Google's incentives have mostly about building models for their products, which makes them focus more on the cheap end, and while they need that, it feels like the Innovator's Dilemma is biting them here.

I own a lot of Google stock from working there in the past and have been quite happy about their trajectory up until the last 6 months, but I am getting pretty antsy about their AI story these days.

sentences.en: I don't think it's that early tbh, agentic coding has ~90% adoption in the US.Claude Code has largely won individual developer mindshare and has been on top ever since it came out.
sentences.zh: 我不认为早期代理编码在美国的采用率约为 90%。Claude Code 在很大程度上赢得了个人开发人员的关注，并且自推出以来一直处于领先地位。

sentences.en: The benchmarks change, but almost nobody opts to use anything other than Claude IME when I ask them.
sentences.zh: 基准发生了变化，但当我问他们时，几乎没有人选择使用 Claude IME 以外的任何东西。

sentences.en: Enterprise is more competitive since they care about costs and other things, but developers leaning towards Claude puts a thumb on the scales there.The product doesn't have much lock in, so it is possible to dislodge Claude, and Anthropic could (and some may argue is likely to) just shoot themselves in the foot again and again and again, but Google has never been particularly good at enterprise sales, and they have never actually been at the frontier of intelligence.I think Google's incentives have mostly about building models for their products, which makes them focus more on the cheap end, and while they need that, it feels like the Innovator's Dilemma is biting them here.I own a lot of Google stock from working there in the past and have been quite happy about their trajectory up until the last 6 months, but I am getting pretty antsy about their AI story these days.
sentences.zh: 企业更具竞争力，因为他们关心成本和其他事情，但倾向于 Claude 的开发人员将拇指放在了那里的天平上。该产品没有太多锁定，因此有可能驱逐 Claude，而 Anthropic 可能（有些人可能会认为可能）一次又一次搬起石头砸自己的脚，但谷歌从来没有特别擅长企业销售，而且他们实际上从未处于智能的前沿。我认为谷歌的激励措施主要是为他们的产品建立模型的产品，这让他们更加关注廉价产品，虽然他们需要这一点，但感觉创新者的困境正在咬住他们。我过去在那里工作，持有大量谷歌股票，直到过去 6 个月，我对他们的发展轨迹感到非常满意，但这些天我对他们的人工智能故事感到非常不安。

The benchmarks change, but almost nobody opts to use anything other than Claude IME when I ask them. Enterprise is more competitive since they care about costs and other things, but developers leaning towards Claude puts a thumb on the scales there.

The product doesn't have much lock in, so it is possible to dislodge Claude, and Anthropic could (and some may argue is likely to) just shoot themselves in the foot again and again and again, but Google has never been particularly good at enterprise sales, and they have never actually been at the frontier of intelligence.

I think Google's incentives have mostly about building models for their products, which makes them focus more on the cheap end, and while they need that, it feels like the Innovator's Dilemma is biting them here.

I own a lot of Google stock from working there in the past and have been quite happy about their trajectory up until the last 6 months, but I am getting pretty antsy about their AI story these days.

blocks.html_en:
> Claude Code has largely won individual developer mindshare and has been on top ever since it came out.

Claude Code's success is not due to the agent but because the model is considered the best for programming and is very heavily subsidized, compared to pay as you go API prices. Consumers and Enterprise are not really locked in and will go where it makes the most sense.

I think they have almost no loyalty by actual developers.

blocks.html_en:
This isn't a perfect survey, but I am a Codex user and most of my employees are pretty stuck in their workflows and were not really interested in trying even when I was saying good things (even pre-Opus 4.8).

When we work trial people, 100% of people ask for Claude rather than Codex or anything else.

When I talk to people at non-AI tech events everyone basically says they use Claude and have not tried an alternative.

Developers writ large are actually not that interested in trying multiple tools, they like customizing their chosen tool and tweaking it forever.

I think developers are as susceptible to brand marketing as everyone else. It's why almost everyone has a Macbook.

sentences.en: This isn't a perfect survey, but I am a Codex user and most of my employees are pretty stuck in their workflows and were not really interested in trying even when I was saying good things (even pre-Opus 4.8).When we work trial people, 100% of people ask for Claude rather than Codex or anything else.When I talk to people at non-AI tech events everyone basically says they use Claude and have not tried an alternative.Developers writ large are actually not that interested in trying multiple tools, they like customizing their chosen tool and tweaking it forever.I think developers are as susceptible to brand marketing as everyone else.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个完美的调查，但我是一名 Codex 用户，我的大多数员工都非常困在他们的工作流程中，即使我在说好话（甚至是 Opus 4.8 之前的版本），他们也没有真正的兴趣去尝试。当我们对试用人员进行工作时，100% 的人要求使用 Claude，而不是 Codex 或其他任何东西。当我在非 AI 技术活动中与人们交谈时，每个人基本上都说他们使用 Claude，并且没有尝试过替代方案。显然，开发人员实际上并没有兴趣尝试。多种工具，他们喜欢定制自己选择的工具并永远对其进行调整。我认为开发人员和其他人一样容易受到品牌营销的影响。

sentences.en: It's why almost everyone has a Macbook.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么几乎每个人都拥有一台 Macbook。

There is no reason to be loyal....There is no moat.

Basically you may choose to drink brand A water bottle, brand B water bottle or tap water. Oh and you might choose the glass water bottle if you use API/Fable.

blocks.html_en: Nah, these harnesses like Claude Code and Codex are very sticky, I don't see many coworkers switching often, as they have set workflows for their particular harness. It's like vim and emacs, once you pick one it's unlikely you'd switch especially as the models are all "good enough" by now.

blocks.html_en: Every model has its strengths and weaknesses, being loyal is suboptimal unless you mean being loyal to all of them, which is why cursor would have been well positioned before it got acquired. Now you have to jump through hoops to call Gemini from Claude from codex. Yuck.

sentences.en: Every model has its strengths and weaknesses, being loyal is suboptimal unless you mean being loyal to all of them, which is why cursor would have been well positioned before it got acquired.

Claude Code (or any other model/infra/harness co-design) is not subsidized in any normal use of that word. It's trough filling (I've written this up in lurid detail so I'm only going to do it again if anyone cares).

It's not true, it's just a play for margin.

blocks.html_en: Early days or not, Google fucking deleted my IDE and wiped my settings. It took them days to roll out a fix, by which point I had migrated off Antigravity.

sentences.en: Early days or not, Google fucking deleted my IDE and wiped my settings.
sentences.zh: 不管是不是早期，Google 他妈的删除了我的 IDE 并擦除了我的设置。

sentences.en: It took them days to roll out a fix, by which point I had migrated off Antigravity.
sentences.zh: 他们花了几天时间才推出修复程序，此时我已经脱离了反重力。

blocks.html_en: I don't think that's true if the reason a company has left the vendor for given model by making it hard to buy. Enterprise IT is enough of a pain in the butt that people will forego the new shiny to avoid the old painful unless it's genuinely better. As you say though, the best frontier model flips regularly, so companies won't go through the hassle of deploying a model if it's proved horrible to do in the past. They'll just skip that model because their current one is fine.

sentences.en: I don't think that's true if the reason a company has left the vendor for given model by making it hard to buy.
sentences.zh: 如果一家公司因为某种型号而离开供应商的原因是让其难以购买，我认为这并不正确。

sentences.en: Enterprise IT is enough of a pain in the butt that people will forego the new shiny to avoid the old painful unless it's genuinely better.
sentences.zh: 企业 IT 已经够让人头疼的了，以至于人们会放弃新的闪亮的东西来避免旧的痛苦，除非它真的更好。

sentences.en: As you say though, the best frontier model flips regularly, so companies won't go through the hassle of deploying a model if it's proved horrible to do in the past.
sentences.zh: 但正如你所说，最好的前沿模型会定期翻转，因此，如果过去证明该模型的实施很糟糕，公司就不会经历部署模型的麻烦。

sentences.en: They'll just skip that model because their current one is fine.
sentences.zh: 他们会跳过该模型，因为他们当前的模型很好。

blocks.html_en: I guess? Maybe I'm alone here but I don't feel like Fable is particularly more useful than Sonnet most of the time. I feel like the LLMs are good enough for the majority of uses and the hyper expensive premium ones are way into diminishing returns. At this point with Kimi being as good as it is, if they jack up the price any more I'll just go open source.

sentences.en: I guess?
sentences.zh: 我猜？

sentences.en: Maybe I'm alone here but I don't feel like Fable is particularly more useful than Sonnet most of the time.
sentences.zh: 也许我一个人在这里，但我并不觉得寓言在大多数时候比十四行诗特别有用。

sentences.en: I feel like the LLMs are good enough for the majority of uses and the hyper expensive premium ones are way into diminishing returns.
sentences.zh: 我觉得法学硕士足以满足大多数用途，而超昂贵的高级法学硕士却会导致收益递减。

sentences.en: At this point with Kimi being as good as it is, if they jack up the price any more I'll just go open source.
sentences.zh: 现在 Kimi 已经很优秀了，如果他们再抬高价格，我就会选择开源。

blocks.html_en:
Model quality is only one aspect, the bigger problem is making it work in a fully integrated enterprise platform, and Google has always been lacking when it came to the latter.

At this rate, if Google has a flagship model, you're better off plugging it into a competitor's tooling than hope Google figures out how to use it.

sentences.en: Model quality is only one aspect, the bigger problem is making it work in a fully integrated enterprise platform, and Google has always been lacking when it came to the latter.At this rate, if Google has a flagship model, you're better off plugging it into a competitor's tooling than hope Google figures out how to use it.
sentences.zh: 模型质量只是一方面，更大的问题是让它在完全集成的企业平台上运行，而谷歌在后者方面一直有所欠缺。按照这个速度，如果谷歌有旗舰模型，你最好将它插入竞争对手的工具中，而不是希望谷歌弄清楚如何使用它。

blocks.html_en:
Google Gemini agy is not allowing you to use your token via your own harness. My own harness is far more efficient than agy. They can take a simple stance - if you exceed your token limit they block you - with the 5 hr limit they are already doing this . so there should be no reason to block you from using your own harness - if you are more efficient - you gain - if you are less you lose .

They are not allowing me to hit their endpoints which agy hits - it's frustrating . i tried to hack it with gemini itself. what i love about gemini is it's so encouraging and ready to help you - even against the agy client : ) .

Even though im so frustrated with this - i still love Gemini for some reason ! Most encouraging model in the world!

sentences.en: Google Gemini agy is not allowing you to use your token via your own harness.
sentences.zh: Google Gemini agy 不允许您通过自己的工具使用令牌。

sentences.en: My own harness is far more efficient than agy.
sentences.zh: 我自己的安全带比阿吉效率高得多。

sentences.en: They can take a simple stance - if you exceed your token limit they block you - with the 5 hr limit they are already doing this .
sentences.zh: 他们可以采取简单的立场 - 如果您超过令牌限制，他们会阻止您 - 在 5 小时限制下他们已经这样做了。

sentences.en: so there should be no reason to block you from using your own harness - if you are more efficient - you gain - if you are less you lose .They are not allowing me to hit their endpoints which agy hits - it's frustrating .
sentences.zh: 所以没有理由阻止你使用自己的安全带 - 如果你更有效率 - 你会获得 - 如果你更少，你就会失去。他们不允许我达到他们的终点，而 agy 却达到了 - 这是令人沮丧的。

sentences.en: i tried to hack it with gemini itself.
sentences.zh: 我试图用 Gemini 本身来破解它。

sentences.en: what i love about gemini is it's so encouraging and ready to help you - even against the agy client : ) .Even though im so frustrated with this - i still love Gemini for some reason !
sentences.zh: 我喜欢双子座的一点是，它是如此令人鼓舞，并且随时准备帮助您 - 即使是面对年迈的客户:)。尽管我对此感到非常沮丧 - 由于某种原因，我仍然喜欢双子座！

sentences.en: Most encouraging model in the world!
sentences.zh: 世界上最鼓舞人心的模特！

blocks.html_en: Google Gemini agy is not allowing you to use your token via your own harness. My own harness is far more efficient than agy. They can take a simple stance - if you exceed your token limit they block you - with the 5 hr limit they are already doing this . so there should be no reason to block you from using your own harness - if you are more efficient - you gain - if you are less you lose .

sentences.en: Google Gemini agy is not allowing you to use your token via your own harness.
sentences.zh: Google Gemini agy 不允许您通过自己的工具使用令牌。

sentences.en: My own harness is far more efficient than agy.
sentences.zh: 我自己的安全带比阿吉效率高得多。

sentences.en: They can take a simple stance - if you exceed your token limit they block you - with the 5 hr limit they are already doing this .
sentences.zh: 他们可以采取简单的立场 - 如果您超过令牌限制，他们会阻止您 - 在 5 小时限制下他们已经这样做了。

sentences.en: so there should be no reason to block you from using your own harness - if you are more efficient - you gain - if you are less you lose .
sentences.zh: 因此，没有理由阻止您使用自己的安全带 - 如果您效率更高，您就会获得收益；如果您效率较低，您就会损失。

blocks.html_en: They are not allowing me to hit their endpoints which agy hits - it's frustrating . i tried to hack it with gemini itself. what i love about gemini is it's so encouraging and ready to help you - even against the agy client : ) .

sentences.en: They are not allowing me to hit their endpoints which agy hits - it's frustrating .
sentences.zh: 他们不允许我达到他们的终点，而阿吉却达到了终点——这令人沮丧。

sentences.en: i tried to hack it with gemini itself.
sentences.zh: 我试图用 Gemini 本身来破解它。

sentences.en: what i love about gemini is it's so encouraging and ready to help you - even against the agy client : ) .
sentences.zh: 我喜欢双子座的一点是，它非常鼓舞人心，随时准备帮助您 - 即使是针对 agy 客户:)。

blocks.html_en: Even though im so frustrated with this - i still love Gemini for some reason ! Most encouraging model in the world!

sentences.en: Even though im so frustrated with this - i still love Gemini for some reason !
sentences.zh: 尽管我对此感到非常沮丧 - 出于某种原因我仍然爱双子座！

sentences.en: Most encouraging model in the world!
sentences.zh: 世界上最鼓舞人心的模特！

blocks.html_en: Aren’t the subscriptions extremely subsidized and burning cash for Anthropic and OpenAI? A reasonable explanation is they’re simply abstaining from the war of attrition, especially given cheaper comparable models are breaking the illusion that the “frontier of intelligence” has any kind of per token margin.

sentences.en: Aren’t the subscriptions extremely subsidized and burning cash for Anthropic and OpenAI?
sentences.zh: Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的订阅不是得到了极大的补贴和烧钱吗？

sentences.en: A reasonable explanation is they’re simply abstaining from the war of attrition, especially given cheaper comparable models are breaking the illusion that the “frontier of intelligence” has any kind of per token margin.
sentences.zh: 一个合理的解释是，他们只是放弃了消耗战，特别是考虑到更便宜的同类模型正在打破“情报前沿”具有任何代币利润的幻想。

blocks.html_en:
This is hotly debated and completely unclear. Let's say Anthropics Opus models cost the same to serve as GLM 5.2. GLM 5.2 is 4.4$/MTok while Opus is 5.6 times more expensive. Assume that GLM 5.2 is served at essentially zero margin. Then Anthropic has >80% margin on API pricing. So even if an average person with a subscription pays only 20% of the API price of their usage, Anthropic makes money on subscriptions.

And the real numbers could be better for Anthropic. It's feasible Opus models are actually cheaper to serve than GLM 5.2 because Anthropic have optimized the hell out of inference.

sentences.en: This is hotly debated and completely unclear.
sentences.zh: 这引起了激烈的争论，而且完全不清楚。

sentences.en: Let's say Anthropics Opus models cost the same to serve as GLM 5.2.
sentences.zh: 假设 Anthropics Opus 模型的成本与 GLM 5.2 相同。

sentences.en: GLM 5.2 is 4.4$/MTok while Opus is 5.6 times more expensive.
sentences.zh: GLM 5.2 为 4.4 美元/MTok，而 Opus 则贵 5.6 倍。

sentences.en: Assume that GLM 5.2 is served at essentially zero margin.
sentences.zh: 假设 GLM 5.2 的保证金基本为零。

sentences.en: Then Anthropic has >80% margin on API pricing.
sentences.zh: 然后，Anthropic 在 API 定价上的利润率超过 80%。

sentences.en: So even if an average person with a subscription pays only 20% of the API price of their usage, Anthropic makes money on subscriptions.And the real numbers could be better for Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 因此，即使订阅的普通人只支付其使用的 API 价格的 20%，Anthropic 也可以通过订阅赚钱。而且对于 Anthropic 来说，实际数字可能会更好。

sentences.en: It's feasible Opus models are actually cheaper to serve than GLM 5.2 because Anthropic have optimized the hell out of inference.
sentences.zh: 这是可行的 Opus 模型实际上比 GLM 5.2 的服务成本更便宜，因为 Anthropic 已经对推理进行了彻底的优化。

blocks.html_en:
I know many, many engineers who are paying something like 2-5% (via subscription) of what their usage would cost if billed by API tokens.

I know some down to about 1% ($200 Max plan vs $20k in tokens per month)

blocks.html_en:
And I know many people that don't. That have a 20 or 100 dollar subscription for very bursty workflows with months where they barely use tokens.

Not every subscriber is a full time SWE. In fact most professional SWEs will be on enterprise plans and thus not get subscriptions at all.

I think it's very plausible that subscriptions are overall losing money. But we simply don't know.

blocks.html_en:
Sure, but then why wouldn’t I use GLM 5.2 at cost or K3?

I guess that’s the big question, will people pay a big margin long term to use their end products / models or will AI tokens be commoditized by many competing players. For coding if I had to pay API costs I’d switch in a heartbeat, enterprise maybe more reluctant?

sentences.en: Sure, but then why wouldn’t I use GLM 5.2 at cost or K3?I guess that’s the big question, will people pay a big margin long term to use their end products / models or will AI tokens be commoditized by many competing players.
sentences.zh: 当然可以，但是为什么我不按成本使用 GLM 5.2 或 K3？我想这是一个大问题，人们会长期支付大量利润来使用他们的最终产品/模型，还是 AI 代币会被许多竞争者商品化。

sentences.en: For coding if I had to pay API costs I’d switch in a heartbeat, enterprise maybe more reluctant?
sentences.zh: 对于编码，如果我必须支付 API 成本，我会立即切换，企业可能更不愿意？

blocks.html_en: I think the subscriptions pay them back in spades because the same dev who maxes out their subscription on their personal account transfers that exact behaviour over to their enterprise work and - guess what - it's all billed per token there. This is a large reason why corporates are reeling from the cost right now, I think.

sentences.en: I think the subscriptions pay them back in spades because the same dev who maxes out their subscription on their personal account transfers that exact behaviour over to their enterprise work and - guess what - it's all billed per token there.
sentences.zh: 我认为订阅给他们带来了丰厚的回报，因为同一个开发人员在其个人帐户上最大化了他们的订阅，并将该确切行为转移到他们的企业工作中，并且 - 你猜怎么着 - 那里的所有费用都是按代币计费的。

sentences.en: This is a large reason why corporates are reeling from the cost right now, I think.
sentences.zh: 我认为，这是企业目前因成本而陷入困境的一个重要原因。

sentences.zh: 我认为订阅给他们带来了丰厚的回报，因为同一个开发人员在其个人帐户上最大化了他们的订阅，并将该确切行为转移到他们的企业工作中，并且 - 你猜怎么着 - 那里的所有费用都是按代币计费的。

sentences.en: This is a large reason why corporates are reeling from the cost right now, I think.
sentences.zh: 我认为，这是企业目前因成本而陷入困境的一个重要原因。

blocks.html_en:
I am going to ask a very direct question and only because I am curious.

Why do you care? Why would you spend your own money to a multi trillion dollar company so that they win their own "war" against another multi trillion dollar company?

Please don't get me wrong, I know the question can seem a bit negative, I am really just curious.

No, it's a fair question. The answer: I believe(d) in Demis Hassabis's vision of AI

Although, I think saying 'wanted them to win' was not accurate. More like, I stuck with them hoping it will get better, and it did get better in many ways, coding was not one of them.

sentences.en: More like, I stuck with them hoping it will get better, and it did get better in many ways, coding was not one of them.
sentences.zh: 更像是，我坚持他们，希望它会变得更好，而且它确实在很多方面都变得更好，编码不是其中之一。

I trust Google to:

* Abruptly ban me and all users from a Google Workspace for no reason whatsoever.

* Abruptly shut down a service.

I'll never give Google my direct money.

blocks.html_en:
Life gets simpler and better as soon as you stop giving money to Google.

It’s so silly that individual people still use their shit. Corporations, I understand - they always choose the most mediocre stacks and tools by default. But why people choose to bring the mediocrity of Google into their lives is beyond me.

sentences.en: Life gets simpler and better as soon as you stop giving money to Google.It’s so silly that individual people still use their shit.
sentences.zh: 一旦你停止向谷歌提供资金，生活就会变得更简单、更好。这太愚蠢了，以至于个人仍然在使用他们的狗屎。

sentences.en: Corporations, I understand - they always choose the most mediocre stacks and tools by default.

sentences.en: But why people choose to bring the mediocrity of Google into their lives is beyond me.
sentences.zh: 但我不明白为什么人们选择将谷歌的平庸带入他们的生活。

blocks.html_en: That’s one braindead strawman. There’s millions of apps to choose from on your phone. Alternatives to Google are decades old. But do enjoy getting your ass profiled in gmail and let me know if the ads in your mailbox are helpfully tailored to your needs and preferences!

> they left us reeling with their abrupt product decisions

This is what happens when you put a McKinsey consultant in the role of CEO of an organization where product managers run the asylum instead of engineers.

blocks.html_en:
It's funny that they triggered the infamous "Code Red" moment in OpenAI when the 3-3.1 models came out. I switched to using them for a lot of single-shot LLM calls because they were fast and cheap. Their only area that they were lacking in was agentic/tool calling.

Needless to say 3.5 was a disappointment. Curious to see 3.6.

sentences.zh: 有趣的是，当 3-3.1 模型出现时，他们引发了 OpenAI 中臭名昭著的“红色代码”时刻。

sentences.en: I switched to using them for a lot of single-shot LLM calls because they were fast and cheap.
sentences.zh: 我转而使用它们来进行大量的一次性 LLM 调用，因为它们快速且便宜。

sentences.zh: 他们唯一缺乏的领域是代理/工具调用。不用说 3.5 是令人失望的。

sentences.en: Curious to see 3.6.
sentences.zh: 很好奇看到3.6。

blocks.html_en:
> Google somehow managed to snatch defeat from the jaws of success with their AI products.

HN lives in a bubble.

I have German/Italian/Polish clients virtually all use Gemini and NotebookLM. Talking insurance, banking, consulting, legal.

The real world doesn't look at pointless benchmarks on writing react tailwind crap, they are already google suite users, get the tools, test them and adopt them, end of story.

It's going to be like with angular, never mentioned on the net, widely used in the real world.

sentences.en: > Google somehow managed to snatch defeat from the jaws of success with their AI products.HN lives in a bubble.I have German/Italian/Polish clients virtually all use Gemini and NotebookLM.
sentences.zh: > 谷歌以某种方式设法通过他们的人工智能产品从成功的魔爪中夺回失败。HN 生活在泡沫中。我的德国/意大利/波兰客户几乎都使用 Gemini 和 NotebookLM。

sentences.en: Talking insurance, banking, consulting, legal.The real world doesn't look at pointless benchmarks on writing react tailwind crap, they are already google suite users, get the tools, test them and adopt them, end of story.It's going to be like with angular, never mentioned on the net, widely used in the real world.
sentences.zh: 谈论保险、银行、咨询、法律。现实世界不会关注编写反应顺风垃圾的毫无意义的基准，他们已经是谷歌套件用户，获取工具，测试它们并采用它们，故事结束。这将像角度一样，从未在网络上提及，在现实世界中广泛使用。

blocks.html_en:
Yup, it is often mind-boggling how people here are detached from reality. Millions of people around the world rely on Google services, Gemini in all probability is the most used model consumer-wise, Google receives unimaginable stream of data on even smallest habits common people have etc.

But hey, Gemini makes shittier job than Fable at producing my shitty "app" no one cares about. It is so over for Google!

sentences.en: Yup, it is often mind-boggling how people here are detached from reality.
sentences.zh: 是的，这里的人们与现实的脱节常常令人难以置信。

sentences.en: Millions of people around the world rely on Google services, Gemini in all probability is the most used model consumer-wise, Google receives unimaginable stream of data on even smallest habits common people have etc.But hey, Gemini makes shittier job than Fable at producing my shitty "app" no one cares about.
sentences.zh: 全世界有数百万人依赖 Google 服务，Gemini 很可能是消费者方面最常用的模式，Google 收到了难以想象的数据流，即使是普通人最小的习惯等等。但是，嘿，Gemini 在制作我的烂“应用程序”方面做得比寓言更糟糕，没人关心。

sentences.en: It is so over for Google!
sentences.zh: 谷歌就这样结束了！

blocks.html_en:
"Your new models are great, but uh... How exactly am I supposed to use them?"

That is, given a lot of users' contexts, the way they can and the way they want to use these models are increasingly disjoint.

sentences.en: "Your new models are great, but uh...
sentences.zh: “你的新模型很棒，但是呃......

sentences.en: How exactly am I supposed to use them?"That is, given a lot of users' contexts, the way they can and the way they want to use these models are increasingly disjoint.
sentences.zh: 我究竟应该如何使用它们？”也就是说，考虑到很多用户的情况，他们可以使用这些模型的方式和他们想要使用这些模型的方式越来越脱节。

blocks.html_en:
+1. First thing I look for in a model announcement thread. I actually came across this one an hour ago and was sad there were no pelicans yet.

It's a decent heuristic because the better models generate better pelicans. That's all. Nobody sane is going to make a bet on a model based on a pelican. But it's cool, it's tradition by now, and it's a semblance of a good first impression for new models.

sentences.en: First thing I look for in a model announcement thread.
sentences.zh: 我在模型公告线程中寻找的第一件事。

sentences.en: I actually came across this one an hour ago and was sad there were no pelicans yet.It's a decent heuristic because the better models generate better pelicans.
sentences.zh: 事实上，我一小时前就遇到过这个，很遗憾还没有鹈鹕。这是一个不错的启发式方法，因为更好的模型会产生更好的鹈鹕。

sentences.en: That's all.
sentences.zh: 就这样。

sentences.en: Nobody sane is going to make a bet on a model based on a pelican.
sentences.zh: 任何理智的人都不会在基于鹈鹕的模型上打赌。

sentences.en: But it's cool, it's tradition by now, and it's a semblance of a good first impression for new models.
sentences.zh: 但这很酷，现在已经成为传统，而且这似乎给新车型带来了良好的第一印象。

Imo it's a very stupid test, and should not be used for model performance.

But you bet my ass I check everytime to have a look at see how that pelican looks, it's just a fun check and also interesting to see the cost/results.

sentences.en: Imo it's a very stupid test, and should not be used for model performance.But you bet my ass I check everytime to have a look at see how that pelican looks, it's just a fun check and also interesting to see the cost/results.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这是一个非常愚蠢的测试，不应该用于模型性能。但是你敢打赌，我每次都会检查看看那只鹈鹕的样子，这只是一个有趣的检查，而且看到成本/结果也很有趣。

blocks.html_en:
Thank you for posting them (as well as all your other AI takes). It's appreciated here!

I feel like the human brain massively overweights negative feedback over positive, and that's even after accounting for the fact that internet discourse tends to mostly surface negative comments (whereas the enjoyers stay silent). By default I have to try hard not to take it personally whenever someone leaves a negative comment about my work.

So just doing my bit to say I appreciate your commentary on so much of the fast-evolving AI landscape. Helps me orient :)

sentences.en: Thank you for posting them (as well as all your other AI takes).
sentences.zh: 感谢您发布它们（以及您所有其他人工智能拍摄的内容）。

sentences.en: It's appreciated here!I feel like the human brain massively overweights negative feedback over positive, and that's even after accounting for the fact that internet discourse tends to mostly surface negative comments (whereas the enjoyers stay silent).
sentences.zh: 在此表示赞赏！我觉得人类的大脑严重重视负面反馈而不是正面反馈，即使考虑到互联网话语往往主要表面负面评论（而享受者保持沉默）这一事实也是如此。

sentences.en: By default I have to try hard not to take it personally whenever someone leaves a negative comment about my work.So just doing my bit to say I appreciate your commentary on so much of the fast-evolving AI landscape.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，每当有人对我的工作留下负面评论时，我都必须努力不把它视为针对我个人的。因此，我只是尽力表示，我很欣赏您对快速发展的人工智能领域的评论。

sentences.en: Helps me orient :)
sentences.zh: 帮助我确定方向:)

blocks.html_en: If enough people agreed with you, simonw's top level comment would be grey. It isn't. Not every comment is for every person, that is fine and normal. He didnt hijack a popular thread in here to make his post. He doesnt have a tin can in your face shouting from a soapbox that makes it hard to ignore. Flag or ignore are reasonable options that can be used.

sentences.en: If enough people agreed with you, simonw's top level comment would be grey.
sentences.zh: 如果有足够多的人同意你的观点，simonw 的顶级评论将会是灰色的。

sentences.en: It isn't.
sentences.zh: 事实并非如此。

sentences.en: Not every comment is for every person, that is fine and normal.
sentences.zh: 并不是每个评论都适合每个人，这很好也很正常。

sentences.en: He didnt hijack a popular thread in here to make his post.
sentences.zh: 他并没有劫持这里的热门帖子来发帖。

sentences.en: He doesnt have a tin can in your face shouting from a soapbox that makes it hard to ignore.
sentences.zh: 他不会在你面前用一个锡罐在肥皂盒里大喊大叫，这让人很难忽视。

sentences.en: Flag or ignore are reasonable options that can be used.
sentences.zh: 标记或忽略是可以使用的合理选项。

blocks.html_en:
People aren't supposed to upvote or downvote posts for these kinds of reasons here. Most people are downvoting far too much on this website, and it leads to significant echo-chamber dynamics that are worse than even reddit. The pelican test continuing to be taken seriously is a great example of that kind of echo chamber.

"He doesn't have a tin can in your face shouting from a soapbox that makes it hard to ignore."

He metaphorically does because people upvote his pelicans to the top and the ensuing comment threads are massive/bloated. Huge amounts of the readership of this website are lurkers who don't even know how to hide these giant posts. Look at how bloated this very thread is right now!

Also, a lot of people unironically are whining about him because of sour grapes. Pay them what Simon is likely making, give them as much mindshare/attention as Simon gets, and they wouldn't be so mad.

Anti-incumbency bias and anti-elitist attitudes are good actually.

sentences.en: People aren't supposed to upvote or downvote posts for these kinds of reasons here.
sentences.zh: 人们不应该因为这些原因对帖子投赞成票或反对票。

sentences.en: Most people are downvoting far too much on this website, and it leads to significant echo-chamber dynamics that are worse than even reddit.
sentences.zh: 大多数人在这个网站上投了太多的反对票，这导致了明显的回音室动态，甚至比 Reddit 还要糟糕。

sentences.en: The pelican test continuing to be taken seriously is a great example of that kind of echo chamber."He doesn't have a tin can in your face shouting from a soapbox that makes it hard to ignore."He metaphorically does because people upvote his pelicans to the top and the ensuing comment threads are massive/bloated.
sentences.zh: 持续受到认真对待的鹈鹕测试就是这种回声室的一个很好的例子。“他没有在你面前从肥皂盒中大喊大叫，这让人很难忽视。”他隐喻地这样做是因为人们将他的鹈鹕推到了顶端，随后的评论线程是巨大的/臃肿的。

sentences.en: Huge amounts of the readership of this website are lurkers who don't even know how to hide these giant posts.
sentences.zh: 该网站的大量读者都是潜伏者，他们甚至不知道如何隐藏这些巨大的帖子。

sentences.en: Look at how bloated this very thread is right now!Also, a lot of people unironically are whining about him because of sour grapes.
sentences.zh: 看看现在这个帖子有多臃肿！而且，很多人都讽刺他吃不到葡萄说葡萄酸。

sentences.en: Pay them what Simon is likely making, give them as much mindshare/attention as Simon gets, and they wouldn't be so mad.Anti-incumbency bias and anti-elitist attitudes are good actually.
sentences.zh: 付给他们西蒙可能赚到的钱，给予他们尽可能多的思想分享/关注，他们就不会那么生气。反在职偏见和反精英主义态度实际上是好的。

blocks.html_en:
> People aren't supposed to upvote or downvote posts for these kinds of reasons here.

Agreed, but scrolling past or hitting - were apparently off the table for the complainers. So flagging was yet another tool in their toolbelt and I bet a powerful one at that. If simonw's pelican posts routinely went dead from flagging, he would not make them. You know that, I know that.

> The pelican test continuing to be taken seriously is a great example of that kind of echo chamber.

You can try and support that argument if you like. But I would implore you to realize that it has been had many times recently and the other side does in fact find value and do not see it that way.

> He metaphorically does because people upvote his pelicans to the top and the ensuing comment threads are massive/bloated.

Users upvote the pelicans because they find it interesting. they arent paid trolls or simonw fanatics.

> Huge amounts of the readership of this website are lurkers who don't even know how to hide these giant posts.

They can learn... it's called hackernews. For those interested, that is what the [-] link is for above the comment. Use it and move on.

sentences.en: > People aren't supposed to upvote or downvote posts for these kinds of reasons here.Agreed, but scrolling past or hitting - were apparently off the table for the complainers.
sentences.zh: > 人们不应该因为这些原因对帖子投赞成票或反对票。同意，但滚动过去或点击 - 显然对于抱怨者来说是不可能的。

sentences.en: So flagging was yet another tool in their toolbelt and I bet a powerful one at that.
sentences.zh: 因此，标记是他们工具带中的另一个工具，我敢打赌这是一个强大的工具。

sentences.en: If simonw's pelican posts routinely went dead from flagging, he would not make them.
sentences.zh: 如果西蒙努的鹈鹕帖子经常因为下垂而死掉，他就不会制作它们。

sentences.en: You know that, I know that.> The pelican test continuing to be taken seriously is a great example of that kind of echo chamber.You can try and support that argument if you like.
sentences.zh: 你知道这一点，我也知道这一点。> 继续受到认真对待的鹈鹕测试就是这种回声室的一个很好的例子。如果你愿意，你可以尝试支持这个论点。

sentences.en: But I would implore you to realize that it has been had many times recently and the other side does in fact find value and do not see it that way.> He metaphorically does because people upvote his pelicans to the top and the ensuing comment threads are massive/bloated.Users upvote the pelicans because they find it interesting.
sentences.zh: 但我恳请你意识到，最近已经发生过很多次了，而另一方实际上确实发现了价值，但并不这么认为。>他隐喻地这样做是因为人们将他的鹈鹕推到了顶部，随后的评论线程是巨大/臃肿的。用户投票给鹈鹕是因为他们觉得这很有趣。

sentences.en: they arent paid trolls or simonw fanatics.> Huge amounts of the readership of this website are lurkers who don't even know how to hide these giant posts.They can learn...
sentences.zh: 他们不是付费巨魔或 simonw 狂热分子。> 该网站的大量读者都是潜伏者，他们甚至不知道如何隐藏这些巨大的帖子。他们可以学习...

sentences.en: it's called hackernews.
sentences.zh: 这就是所谓的黑客新闻。

sentences.en: For those interested, that is what the [-] link is for above the comment.
sentences.zh: 对于那些感兴趣的人，这就是评论上方的 [-] 链接。

blocks.html_en:
Well, yeah. No one does.

But, also, as said, you're an industry (and foss!) veteran, so I find it impossible to believe that you haven't had your fair share of baseless bullshit being thrown at you, and with that, you gaining a persona that will not be hit by that, because it clearly knows that it is in fact bullshit.

Unless of course it doesn't really know that with certainty.

As said, I would _love_ to give you the benefit of the doubt, because you might just have a stressful day or whatever, but content marketing is literally your whole thing by now. It is impossible for me to do that with a clean conscience.

Your blog front page currently opens with

> Earlier this month I hosted a fireside chat session at the AI Engineer World’s Fair with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic’s Claude Code team.

That is not what "some rando foss maintainer we are morally obligated to be soft with" does.

But I repeat myself.

blocks.html_en:
I'm a professional blogger now. I still also work on open source software. I'm even fine being called an "influencer" (shudder), but I take offense to accusations of unethical behavior.

I think very hard about the ethics of what I'm doing and how I can best use my "platform" (shudder again) in as constructive a way as possible.

sentences.en: I'm a professional blogger now.
sentences.zh: 我现在是一名专业博主。

sentences.en: I still also work on open source software.
sentences.zh: 我仍然致力于开源软件的工作。

sentences.en: I'm even fine being called an "influencer" (shudder), but I take offense to accusations of unethical behavior.I think very hard about the ethics of what I'm doing and how I can best use my "platform" (shudder again) in as constructive a way as possible.
sentences.zh: 我什至可以接受被称为“影响者”（不寒而栗），但我对不道德行为的指控感到生气。我非常认真地思考我所做的事情的道德规范，以及如何以尽可能有建设性的方式最好地利用我的“平台”（再次不寒而栗）。

blocks.html_en:
What do you want from him, seriously? Anyone who follows the scene knows that blogging about AI (and participating in conferences etc.) is what Simon does nowadays.

There's nothing shady here. The disclosure is front and center on his About page on his website.

He's not spamming you. It's one short link, sometimes a link to a first-impressions post. It's interesting and useful for me and the other commenters who keep upvoting his comments. Why are you so antagonistic?

sentences.en: What do you want from him, seriously?
sentences.zh: 说真的，你想从他那里得到什么？

sentences.en: It's interesting and useful for me and the other commenters who keep upvoting his comments.
sentences.zh: 这对我和其他不断支持他的评论的评论者来说很有趣也很有用。

sentences.en: Why are you so antagonistic?
sentences.zh: 你为何如此敌对？

blocks.html_en:
But how else am I supposed to know when we've reached AGI, until I see an absolutely flawless pelican?

All of the pelicans so far have had really weird flaws / quirks so I am always a little interested to see how well these models perform at this task, since I've seen all the past pelicans and have some anchoring.

Seeing a truly flawless pelican would tell me that the model has true visual reasoning capabilities as well as good taste.

sentences.en: But how else am I supposed to know when we've reached AGI, until I see an absolutely flawless pelican?All of the pelicans so far have had really weird flaws / quirks so I am always a little interested to see how well these models perform at this task, since I've seen all the past pelicans and have some anchoring.Seeing a truly flawless pelican would tell me that the model has true visual reasoning capabilities as well as good taste.
sentences.zh: 但是，在我看到一只绝对完美的鹈鹕之前，我还怎么知道我们何时达到了 AGI？到目前为止，所有的鹈鹕都有非常奇怪的缺陷/怪癖，所以我总是对这些模型在这项任务上的表现感兴趣，因为我已经见过所有过去的鹈鹕并有一些锚定。看到一只真正完美的鹈鹕会告诉我，该模型具有真正的视觉推理能力和良好的品味。

sentences.zh: 但是，在我看到一只绝对完美的鹈鹕之前，我还怎么知道我们何时达到了 AGI？到目前为止，所有的鹈鹕都有非常奇怪的缺陷/怪癖，所以我总是对这些模型在这项任务上的表现感兴趣，因为我已经见过所有过去的鹈鹕并有一些锚定。看到一只真正完美的鹈鹕会告诉我，该模型具有真正的视觉推理能力和良好的品味。

It's both.

I agree to rednb that at this point it feels like rather obvious brand building, but also, I agree with you that some value is in it.

It does not feel all that authentic though, and it's good to react allergically to lack of authenticity. Bad for a lot of business models, but good for humanity.
